revision.comment: Text replacement - "宇治山田駅" to "Ujiyamada Station"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name= Okitama-no-Kami, Miyabi-no-Kami, Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami (興玉神・宮比神・屋乃波比伎神) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Okitama no Kami & Miyabi no Kami.jpg|270px]]
Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami's former shrine site (January 2016)
The central stone becomes the object of worship for Okitama-no-Kami during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. | location=[[Mie Prefecture|Mie]], [[Ise City|Ise]], Uji Kanda-cho 1, within the Naikū Goshōgū | coordinates={{coord|34|30|43.6|N|136|44|12.7|E|region:JP24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Okitama Shrine}} | deity=Okitama-no-Kami: Okitama-no-Kami
Miyabi-no-Kami: Miyabi-no-Kami
Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami: Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami | Classification=Subordinate shrine of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} | consecration_year={{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}} | MainHall=None }} [[File:Naiku 001.jpg|250px|thumb|The worship hall of Naikū (the three shrines are located behind this area)]] '''Okitama-no-Kami''' (興玉神) is one of the guardian deities enshrined in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine is located within the sacred precincts of Naikū.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56 This article also describes {{ill|Miyabi-no-Kami|ja|宮比神|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神|12=draft|12=draft}}, subordinate shrines within the same precinct. All three shrines lack dedicated structures and are guardian shrines of Naikū. == Overview == The shrines are located within the sacred precincts of Naikū in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Mie Prefecture. As these precincts are off-limits to general visitors, worshippers typically pay their respects from designated areas along the {{ill|Sando|ja|参道|12=draft|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 113 During the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, conducted every 20 years, the three shrines are also relocated in accordance with the main shrine’s reconstruction.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 229 Among the three shrines, Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami are enshrined on the same stone-paved platform, with Okitama-no-Kami facing west and Miyabi-no-Kami facing north. == Okitama-no-Kami == Okitama-no-Kami is Ranked second among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following {{ill|Takimatsuri-no-Kami|ja|滝deity|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is located at the northwest corner of the precinct on a stone-paved platform facing west. During the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft|12=draft}} in June and December and the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} in October, all participants pray earnestly to Okitama-no-Kami for blessings. The enshrined deity, Okitama-no-Kami, is the guardian of Naikū. According to the ''Shinmei Hisyo,'' it is another name for either {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦大神|12=draft|12=draft}} or his descendant {{ill|Ōtano-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the Kamakura period, Okitama-no-Kami was revered as the land deity of Naikū and played a significant role in offerings during major festivals like Kannamesai and Tsukinami-sai. == Miyabi-no-Kami == Miyabi-no-Kami Ranks third among the subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri-no-Kami and Okitama-no-Kami. It is enshrined on the same platform as Okitama-no-Kami but faces north. Records from the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft}} indicate that {{ill|Uchimonoi-fu|ja|内物忌父|12=draft|12=draft}} offered sacred food during rituals.Sakurai (1991): p. 244 Its location in the northwest contrasts with the southeastern placement of {{ill|Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神|12=draft|12=draft}}, where {{ill|Sotomoi-fu|ja|外物忌父|12=draft|12=draft}} performed similar duties. The enshrined deity, Miyabi-no-Kami, is believed to be another name for either {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|大宮売命|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the wife of Sarutahiko. == Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami == Ranked fourth among the subordinate shrines, Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is located on the east side of Naikū’s stone staircase, facing south. It is also enshrined on a stone-paved platform outside the {{ill|Itagaki Shrine|ja|板垣|12=draft|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 114 During the Kamakura period, {{ill|Sotomoi-fu|ja|外物忌父|12=draft|12=draft}} offered sacred food during rituals, contrasting with the duties of Uchimonoi-fu at Miyabi-no-Kami. The deity Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is the guardian of the sacred grounds of Naikū. According to the ''Kojiki,'' this deity may be the same as the ''Hahiki-no-Kami,'' a descendant of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=draft|12=draft}}, a child of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft|12=draft}}. Scholar Sakurai Katsunoshin suggests it is synonymous with ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.''Sakurai (1991): p. 241 == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the ''Enryaku Ritual Book'' of 804 but appear in documents from the late Heian period. The names Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami are consistent, but Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is referred to as ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.'' == Access == To the front of Uji Bridge: * By train: From {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}}, take the Mie Kōtsū bus from {{ill|Iseshi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=draft|12=draft}} to Naikū-mae.Mie Tourism Federation, "Ise Grand Shrine (Naikū)" (accessed September 12, 2013). * By car: From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, exit at Ise West IC or Ise IC and follow signs to parking areas near Naikū. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Gakken Publishing, ''Let’s Visit Ise Grand Shrine,'' supervised by {{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔|12=draft|12=draft}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1. * Sakurai Katsunoshin, ''The Development and Archetype of Ise Grand Shrine,'' Kokusho Publishing, 1991. ISBN 4-336-03296-3. * Fukuyama Toshio et al., ''Jingū: The 60th Shikinen Sengū,'' Shogakukan, 1975. * Ise Bunka-sha, ''Pilgrimage to 125 Shrines of Ise,'' Ise Culture Publishing, 2008. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4. == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shishi Shrine|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0110.html Okitama-no-Kami] - Jingū Association * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0111.html Miyabi-no-Kami] - Jingū Association * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0112.html Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami] - Jingū Association {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Okitama no Kami, Miyabi no Kami, and Yano Wahiki no Kami | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Okitama no Kami & Miyabi no Kami.jpg|270px]]
The ancient shrine site of Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami (2016)
During the {{ill|Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, the central stone is enshrined as Okitama no Kami. | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Uji-Kannabe-cho 1, within the Goshōgu (main sanctuary) of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮|12=draft|12=draft}} | LatitudeDeg = 34 | LatitudeMin = 30 | LatitudeSec = 43.6 | LongitudeDeg = 136 | LongitudeMin = 44 | LongitudeSec = 12.7 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Okitama no Kami, Miyabi no Kami, and Yano Wahiki no Kami | type = Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} | Established = {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}} | Main Building = None | Alias = | Pilgrimage = | festival = | Rituals = }} [[File:Naiku 001.jpg|250px|thumb|The worship hall of Naikū, where the three shrines are located in the rear]] '''Okitama no Kami''' (興玉神) is one of the subordinate shrines under the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the associated deity of the shrine. Located within the inner sanctuary of Naikū, this article also describes {{ill|Miyabi no Kami|ja|宮比神|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yano Wahiki no Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神|12=draft|12=draft}}, two additional subordinate shrines located in the same precinct. All three shrines do not have their own shrine buildings and enshrine their deities directly on stone pavements, serving as protective deities for Naikū.Gakken Publishing (2013): Page 56 == Overview == Located within the inner sanctuary of Naikū in Uji-Kannabe-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. General worshippers cannot enter the inner sanctuary and instead worship from the nearby paths.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): Page 113 During the {{ill|Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, held every 20 years, the three shrines are also moved along with the main sanctuary.Fukuyama et al. (1975): Page 229 Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami are enshrined on the same stone pavement. Okitama no Kami faces west, while Miyabi no Kami faces north. == Okitama no Kami == Okitama no Kami Ranks second among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following {{ill|Takimatsuri no Kami|ja|滝deity|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is enshrined on a west-facing stone pavement located in the northwest corner of the inner sanctuary. During the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft|12=draft}} in June and December, as well as the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} in October, those serving the rituals first offer their prayers with full devotion to Okitama no Kami. The enshrined deity, Okitama no Kami (興玉神), is considered the guardian deity of the main sanctuary. According to the *Jinmei Hisho* (神名秘書), Okitama no Kami is another name for {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=draft|12=draft}} or his descendant {{ill|Ōta no Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=draft|12=draft}}. During the Kamakura period, Okitama no Kami was worshipped as the landowner deity (*jinushi kami*) of the Naikū precincts, and it played a central role in the rituals of the Kannamesai and Tsukinami-no-Matsuri. == Miyabi no Kami == Miyabi no Kami Ranks third among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri no Kami and Okitama no Kami. It is enshrined on a north-facing stone pavement located in the northwest corner of the inner sanctuary. According to records from the {{ill|Kenkū|ja|建久|12=draft|12=draft}} era (1190–1198), {{ill|Uchimonoi chichi|ja|内物忌父|12=draft|12=draft}} and others were responsible for offering sacred food (*shinsen*).Sakurai (1991): Page 244 The enshrined deity, Miyabi no Kami (宮比神), is considered another guardian deity of the main sanctuary. Miyabi no Kami is believed to be an alias of {{ill|Ōmiyanome no Mikoto|ja|大宮売命|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the wife of Sarutahiko Ōkami. == Yano Wahiki no Kami == Yano Wahiki no Kami Ranks fourth among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri no Kami, Okitama no Kami, and Miyabi no Kami. It is enshrined on the south-facing stone pavement located east of the stone steps of the Goshōgu. Like the other two shrines, it is enshrined on a stone pavement and located outside the Itagaki Gate.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): Page 114 The enshrined deity, Yano Wahiki no Kami (屋乃波比伎神), is considered the guardian deity of the sacred grounds surrounding the main sanctuary. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yano Wahiki no Kami is mentioned as a descendant of {{ill|Susanoo no Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft|12=draft}} through his son {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the *Enryaku Gishikichō* (延暦儀式帳) from {{ill|Enryaku 23|ja|延暦23rd year|12=draft|12=draft}} (804), but their names appear in documents from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. The names Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami remain unchanged, but Yano Wahiki no Kami was historically referred to as "Yano Wahiki no Kami" (矢乃波々岐神). == Access == ; To Uji Bridge: * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} Sangu Line or {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} Yamada Line, disembark at {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (south exit) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=draft|12=draft}} and take a Mie Kōtsū bus for approximately 15 minutes to Naikū-mae.Mie Prefecture Tourism Association [http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Shrine (Naikū)] (Accessed September 12, 2013) * Accessible by car from {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} via Ise West IC or Ise IC, with parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okita-no-Kami]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:January]] [[Category:Ise City shrines]] [[Category:興玉神]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Okita-no-Kami]] [[ja:興玉神]] [[en:Okita-no-Kami]]

During the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} in June and December and the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭}} in October, all participants pray earnestly to Okitama-no-Kami for blessings. The enshrined deity, Okitama-no-Kami, is the guardian of Naikū. According to the ''Shinmei Hisyo,'' it is another name for either {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦大神}} or his descendant {{ill|Ōtano-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命}}. Scholar Sakurai Katsunoshin suggests it is synonymous with ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.''Sakurai (1991): p. 241 == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the ''Enryaku Ritual Book'' of 804 but appear in documents from the late Heian period. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4. == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Shishi Shrine|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami}} * {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0110.html Okitama-no-Kami] - Jingū Association * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0111.html Miyabi-no-Kami] - Jingū Association * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0112.html Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami] - Jingū Association {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Okitama no Kami, Miyabi no Kami, and Yano Wahiki no Kami | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Okitama no Kami & Miyabi no Kami.jpg|270px]]
The ancient shrine site of Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami (2016)
During the {{ill|Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}, the central stone is enshrined as Okitama no Kami. | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, Uji-Kannabe-cho 1, within the Goshōgu (main sanctuary) of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮}} | LatitudeDeg = 34 | LatitudeMin = 30 | LatitudeSec = 43.6 | LongitudeDeg = 136 | LongitudeMin = 44 | LongitudeSec = 12.7 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Okitama no Kami, Miyabi no Kami, and Yano Wahiki no Kami | type = Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} | Established = {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Main Building = None | Alias = | Pilgrimage = | festival = | Rituals = }} [[File:Naiku 001.jpg|250px|thumb|The worship hall of Naikū, where the three shrines are located in the rear]] '''Okitama no Kami''' (興玉神) is one of the subordinate shrines under the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, and the associated deity of the shrine. Located within the inner sanctuary of Naikū, this article also describes {{ill|Miyabi no Kami|ja|宮比神}} and {{ill|Yano Wahiki no Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神}}, two additional subordinate shrines located in the same precinct. All three shrines do not have their own shrine buildings and enshrine their deities directly on stone pavements, serving as protective deities for Naikū.Gakken Publishing (2013): Page 56 == Overview == Located within the inner sanctuary of Naikū in Uji-Kannabe-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. General worshippers cannot enter the inner sanctuary and instead worship from the nearby paths.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): Page 113 During the {{ill|Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}, held every 20 years, the three shrines are also moved along with the main sanctuary.Fukuyama et al. (1975): Page 229 Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami are enshrined on the same stone pavement. Okitama no Kami faces west, while Miyabi no Kami faces north. == Okitama no Kami == Okitama no Kami Ranks second among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following {{ill|Takimatsuri no Kami|ja|滝deity}}. It is enshrined on a west-facing stone pavement located in the northwest corner of the inner sanctuary. During the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} in June and December, as well as the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} in October, those serving the rituals first offer their prayers with full devotion to Okitama no Kami. The enshrined deity, Okitama no Kami (興玉神), is considered the guardian deity of the main sanctuary. According to the *Jinmei Hisho* (神名秘書), Okitama no Kami is another name for {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} or his descendant {{ill|Ōta no Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命}}. During the Kamakura period, Okitama no Kami was worshipped as the landowner deity (*jinushi kami*) of the Naikū precincts, and it played a central role in the rituals of the Kannamesai and Tsukinami-no-Matsuri. == Miyabi no Kami == Miyabi no Kami Ranks third among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri no Kami and Okitama no Kami. It is enshrined on a north-facing stone pavement located in the northwest corner of the inner sanctuary. According to records from the {{ill|Kenkū|ja|建久}} era (1190–1198), {{ill|Uchimonoi chichi|ja|内物忌父}} and others were responsible for offering sacred food (*shinsen*).Sakurai (1991): Page 244 The enshrined deity, Miyabi no Kami (宮比神), is considered another guardian deity of the main sanctuary. Miyabi no Kami is believed to be an alias of {{ill|Ōmiyanome no Mikoto|ja|大宮売命}} or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命}}, the wife of Sarutahiko Ōkami. == Yano Wahiki no Kami == Yano Wahiki no Kami Ranks fourth among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri no Kami, Okitama no Kami, and Miyabi no Kami. It is enshrined on the south-facing stone pavement located east of the stone steps of the Goshōgu. Like the other two shrines, it is enshrined on a stone pavement and located outside the Itagaki Gate.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): Page 114 The enshrined deity, Yano Wahiki no Kami (屋乃波比伎神), is considered the guardian deity of the sacred grounds surrounding the main sanctuary. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Yano Wahiki no Kami is mentioned as a descendant of {{ill|Susanoo no Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}} through his son {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami}}. == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the *Enryaku Gishikichō* (延暦儀式帳) from {{ill|Enryaku 23|ja|延暦23rd year}} (804), but their names appear in documents from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. The names Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami remain unchanged, but Yano Wahiki no Kami was historically referred to as "Yano Wahiki no Kami" (矢乃波々岐神). == Access == ; To Uji Bridge: * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} Sangu Line or {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道}} Yamada Line, disembark at {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} (south exit) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station}} and take a Mie Kōtsū bus for approximately 15 minutes to Naikū-mae.Mie Prefecture Tourism Association [http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Shrine (Naikū)] (Accessed September 12, 2013) * Accessible by car from {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} via Ise West IC or Ise IC, with parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okita-no-Kami]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:January]] [[Category:Ise City shrines]] [[Category:興玉神]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Okita-no-Kami]] [[ja:興玉神]] [[en:Okita-no-Kami]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name= Okitama-no-Kami, Miyabi-no-Kami, Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami (興玉神・宮比神・屋乃波比伎神) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Okitama no Kami & Miyabi no Kami.jpg|270px]]
Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami's former shrine site (January 2016)
The central stone becomes the object of worship for Okitama-no-Kami during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}. | location=[[Mie Prefecture|Mie]], [[Ise City|Ise]], Uji Kanda-cho 1, within the Naikū Goshōgū | coordinates={{coord|34|30|43.6|N|136|44|12.7|E|region:JP24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Okitama Shrine}} | deity=Okitama-no-Kami: Okitama-no-Kami
Miyabi-no-Kami: Miyabi-no-Kami
Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami: Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami | Classification=Subordinate shrine of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} | consecration_year={{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | MainHall=None }} [[File:Naiku 001.jpg|250px|thumb|The worship hall of Naikū (the three shrines are located behind this area)]] '''Okitama-no-Kami''' (興玉神) is one of the guardian deities enshrined in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. The shrine is located within the sacred precincts of Naikū.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56 This article also describes {{ill|Miyabi-no-Kami|ja|宮比神}} and {{ill|Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神}}, subordinate shrines within the same precinct. All three shrines lack dedicated structures and are guardian shrines of Naikū. == Overview == The shrines are located within the sacred precincts of Naikū in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, Mie Prefecture. As these precincts are off-limits to general visitors, worshippers typically pay their respects from designated areas along the {{ill|Sando|ja|参道}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 113 During the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}, conducted every 20 years, the three shrines are also relocated in accordance with the main shrine’s reconstruction.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 229 Among the three shrines, Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami are enshrined on the same stone-paved platform, with Okitama-no-Kami facing west and Miyabi-no-Kami facing north. == Okitama-no-Kami == Okitama-no-Kami is Ranked second among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following {{ill|Takimatsuri-no-Kami|ja|滝deity}}. It is located at the northwest corner of the precinct on a stone-paved platform facing west. During the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} in June and December and the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭}} in October, all participants pray earnestly to Okitama-no-Kami for blessings. The enshrined deity, Okitama-no-Kami, is the guardian of Naikū. According to the ''Shinmei Hisyo,'' it is another name for either {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦大神}} or his descendant {{ill|Ōtano-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命}}. In the Kamakura period, Okitama-no-Kami was revered as the land deity of Naikū and played a significant role in offerings during major festivals like Kannamesai and Tsukinami-sai. == Miyabi-no-Kami == Miyabi-no-Kami Ranks third among the subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri-no-Kami and Okitama-no-Kami. It is enshrined on the same platform as Okitama-no-Kami but faces north. Records from the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} indicate that {{ill|Uchimonoi-fu|ja|内物忌父}} offered sacred food during rituals.Sakurai (1991): p. 244 Its location in the northwest contrasts with the southeastern placement of {{ill|Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神}}, where {{ill|Sotomoi-fu|ja|外物忌父}} performed similar duties. The enshrined deity, Miyabi-no-Kami, is believed to be another name for either {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|大宮売命}} or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命}}, the wife of Sarutahiko. == Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami == Ranked fourth among the subordinate shrines, Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is located on the east side of Naikū’s stone staircase, facing south. It is also enshrined on a stone-paved platform outside the {{ill|Itagaki Shrine|ja|板垣}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 114 During the Kamakura period, {{ill|Sotomoi-fu|ja|外物忌父}} offered sacred food during rituals, contrasting with the duties of Uchimonoi-fu at Miyabi-no-Kami. The deity Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is the guardian of the sacred grounds of Naikū. According to the ''Kojiki,'' this deity may be the same as the ''Hahiki-no-Kami,'' a descendant of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami}}, a child of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}. Scholar Sakurai Katsunoshin suggests it is synonymous with ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.''Sakurai (1991): p. 241 == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the ''Enryaku Ritual Book'' of 804 but appear in documents from the late Heian period. The names Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami are consistent, but Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is referred to as ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.'' == Access == To the front of Uji Bridge: * By train: From {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, take the Mie Kōtsū bus from {{ill|Iseshi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station}} to Naikū-mae.Mie Tourism Federation, "Ise Grand Shrine (Naikū)" (accessed September 12, 2013). * By car: From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, exit at Ise West IC or Ise IC and follow signs to parking areas near Naikū. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Gakken Publishing, ''Let’s Visit Ise Grand Shrine,'' supervised by {{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1. * Sakurai Katsunoshin, ''The Development and Archetype of Ise Grand Shrine,'' Kokusho Publishing, 1991. ISBN 4-336-03296-3. * Fukuyama Toshio et al., ''Jingū: The 60th Shikinen Sengū,'' Shogakukan, 1975. * Ise Bunka-sha, ''Pilgrimage to 125 Shrines of Ise,'' Ise Culture Publishing, 2008. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4. == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Shishi Shrine|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami}} * {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0110.html Okitama-no-Kami] - Jingū Association * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0111.html Miyabi-no-Kami] - Jingū Association * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0112.html Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami] - Jingū Association {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Okitama no Kami, Miyabi no Kami, and Yano Wahiki no Kami | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Okitama no Kami & Miyabi no Kami.jpg|270px]]
The ancient shrine site of Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami (2016)
During the {{ill|Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}, the central stone is enshrined as Okitama no Kami. | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, Uji-Kannabe-cho 1, within the Goshōgu (main sanctuary) of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮}} | LatitudeDeg = 34 | LatitudeMin = 30 | LatitudeSec = 43.6 | LongitudeDeg = 136 | LongitudeMin = 44 | LongitudeSec = 12.7 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Okitama no Kami, Miyabi no Kami, and Yano Wahiki no Kami | type = Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} | Established = {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Main Building = None | Alias = | Pilgrimage = | festival = | Rituals = }} [[File:Naiku 001.jpg|250px|thumb|The worship hall of Naikū, where the three shrines are located in the rear]] '''Okitama no Kami''' (興玉神) is one of the subordinate shrines under the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, and the associated deity of the shrine. According to the *Jinmei Hisho* (神名秘書), Okitama no Kami is another name for {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} or his descendant {{ill|Ōta no Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命}}. revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 興玉神・宮比神・屋乃波比伎神 |画像= [[File:Okitama no Kami & Miyabi no Kami.jpg|270px]]
興玉神と宮比神の古殿地（2016年1月）
[[神宮式年遷宮|遷宮]]が行われると、中央の岩が興玉神として祀られる。 |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]宇治館町1 内宮御正宮内 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 43.6 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 44 | 経度秒 = 12.7 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 興玉神：興玉神
宮比神：宮比神
屋乃波比伎神：屋乃波比伎神 |社格= [[皇大神宮]]末社 |創建= [[平安時代]] |本殿= なし |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= }} [[File:Naiku 001.jpg|250px|thumb|内宮の拝殿（この奥に3社が鎮座する）]] '''興玉神'''（おきたまのかみ）は、[[三重県]][[伊勢市]]に所在する[[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮]]（内宮）の所管社およびその[[祭神]]。内宮の御垣内に鎮座する学研パブリッシング（2013）：56ページ。本項目では、興玉神と同じく正宮御垣内に鎮座する、内宮所管社の'''宮比神'''（みやびのかみ）および'''屋乃波比伎神'''（やのはひきのかみ）についても記述する。 3社とも社殿を持たない[[神社]]であり、[[祭神]]は内宮の[[守護神]]である。 == 概要 == [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]宇治館町、内宮の御垣内に鎮座する。御垣内には一般参拝者が立ち入ることはできないため、付近の[[参道]]から遥拝する伊勢文化舎（2008）：113ページ。内宮の御垣内にあるため、[[神宮式年遷宮|式年遷宮]]で内宮が[[遷御]]するのに合わせて、20年に1度、3社も移動する福山ほか（1975）：229ページ。 3社のうち、興玉神と宮比神は同じ[[石畳]]の上に祀られている。ただし、興玉神は西向きに、宮比神は北向きに鎮座している。 == 興玉神 == 興玉神は、内宮の所管社30社のうち、[[滝祭神]]に次ぐ第2位である。御垣内の西北隅にある石畳の上に西向きに鎮座する。6月と12月の[[月次祭]]および10月の[[神嘗祭]]の際には、奉仕する者全員が、まず興玉神に誠心誠意奉仕することを祈念する。 祭神は社名と同じ興玉神（おきたまのかみ）。正宮の守護神である。『神名秘書』によれば、[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦大神]]またはその[[子孫]]である[[大田命]]の別名であるという。鎌倉時代には宮域の[[地主]]神・猿田彦大神として興玉神を祀り、[[神嘗祭]]と[[月次祭]]の御贄供進の際に祭祀が行われ、重視されてきた。 == 宮比神 == 宮比神は、内宮の所管社30社のうち、滝祭神・興玉神に次いで第3位である。御垣内の西北隅にある石畳の上に北向きに鎮座する。[[建久]]（[[1190年]] - [[1198年]]）の年中行事に関する記録によれば、内物忌父ら（大物忌父・宮守物忌父・地祭物忌父ほか）が[[神饌]]を供する役目を担った櫻井（1991）：244ページ。御垣内の乾（北西）の方角にあり、内物忌父らが奉仕することは、巽（南東）の方角にあり、外物忌父らが奉仕する[[#屋乃波比伎神|屋乃波比伎神]]と対照をなしていた。[[近世]]には外物忌父は絶えており、こうした対照は意味をなさなくなった櫻井（1991）：245ページ。 祭神は社名と同じ宮比神（みやびのかみ）。正宮の守護神である。[[オオミヤノメ|大宮売命（おおみやのめのみこと）]]または猿田彦大神の[[妻]]である[[アメノウズメ|天鈿女命]]の別名であるという。 == 屋乃波比伎神 == 屋乃波比伎神は、内宮の所管社30社のうち、滝祭神・興玉神・宮比神に次ぐ第4位である。御正宮の石階（せっかい）東側に、南向きに鎮座する。興玉神・宮比神と同じく石畳の上に祀られており、板垣御門の外側に鎮座する伊勢文化舎（2008）：114ページ。建久の年中行事に関する記録によれば、外物忌父ら（荒祭物忌父・滝祭物忌父・風宮物忌父ほか）が神饌を供する役目を担った。御垣内の巽（南東）の方角にあり、内物忌父らが奉仕することは、乾（北西）の方角にあり、内物忌父らが奉仕する[[#宮比神|宮比神]]と対照を成していた。近世には外物忌父は絶えており、[[禰宜]]らが「矢乃箒神祭」を奉仕した。 祭神は社名と同じ屋乃波比伎神（やのはひきのかみ）。正宮の神庭の守護神である。『[[古事記]]』には[[須佐之男命]]（すさのおのみこと）の子である[[大年神]]の子の中に「波比伎神」がいる。「矢乃波々岐神」とする文献もあるが、屋乃波比伎神と同じ神であろうと[[櫻井勝之進]]は述べている櫻井（1991）：241ページ。 == 歴史 == [[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）の『延暦儀式帳』には記載されていないが、[[平安時代]]末期の[[文献]]には3社の名前が記されている。興玉神・宮比神は現社名と同じであるが、屋乃波比伎神は「矢乃波々岐神」となっている。 興玉神・宮比神は[[寛文]]9年（[[1669年]]）以降、内宮の内玉垣外の北西の位置に鎮座したが、[[明治2年]]（[[1869年]]/[[1870年]]）からは板垣内北西端に鎮座することとなった。 == 交通 == ;宇治橋前までの交通手段 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]]・[[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄山田線|山田線]][[伊勢市駅]]南口（JR側）または近鉄山田線・[[近鉄鳥羽線|鳥羽線]][[宇治山田駅]]より三重交通バスに15分ほど乗車、内宮前下車三重県観光連盟"[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html 伊勢神宮（内宮）の観光施設・周辺情報 - 観光三重]"（2013年9月12日閲覧。）。近鉄鳥羽線[[五十鈴川駅]]からも三重交通バスで内宮前へ行くことが可能である。 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[伊勢西インターチェンジ|伊勢西IC]]より[[三重県道32号伊勢磯部線]]（御木本道路）を南へ約5分または[[伊勢インターチェンジ|伊勢IC]]より[[国道23号]]を南へ約5分、付近に[[駐車場]]あり。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50年4月20日、246p. * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * [[神宮125社の一覧]] * [[四至神]] * [[二見興玉神社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0110.html 興玉神（おきたまのかみ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0111.html 宮比神（みやびのかみ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0112.html 屋乃波比伎神（やのはひきのかみ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:おきたまのかみ}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:神宮125社]] [[Category:日本の神]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[興玉神]] to [[Okita-no-Kami]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name= Okitama-no-Kami, Miyabi-no-Kami, Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami (興玉神・宮比神・屋乃波比伎神) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Okitama no Kami & Miyabi no Kami.jpg|270px]]
Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami's former shrine site (January 2016)
The central stone becomes the object of worship for Okitama-no-Kami during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}. | location=[[Mie Prefecture|Mie]], [[Ise City|Ise]], Uji Kanda-cho 1, within the Naikū Goshōgū | coordinates={{coord|34|30|43.6|N|136|44|12.7|E|region:JP24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Okitama Shrine}} | deity=Okitama-no-Kami: Okitama-no-Kami
Miyabi-no-Kami: Miyabi-no-Kami
Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami: Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami | Classification=Subordinate shrine of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} | consecration_year={{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | MainHall=None }} [[File:Naiku 001.jpg|250px|thumb|The worship hall of Naikū (the three shrines are located behind this area)]] '''Okitama-no-Kami''' (興玉神) is one of the guardian deities enshrined in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. The shrine is located within the sacred precincts of Naikū.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56 This article also describes {{ill|Miyabi-no-Kami|ja|宮比神}} and {{ill|Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神}}, subordinate shrines within the same precinct. All three shrines lack dedicated structures and are guardian shrines of Naikū. == Overview == The shrines are located within the sacred precincts of Naikū in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, Mie Prefecture. As these precincts are off-limits to general visitors, worshippers typically pay their respects from designated areas along the {{ill|Sando|ja|参道}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 113 During the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}, conducted every 20 years, the three shrines are also relocated in accordance with the main shrine’s reconstruction.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 229 Among the three shrines, Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami are enshrined on the same stone-paved platform, with Okitama-no-Kami facing west and Miyabi-no-Kami facing north. == Okitama-no-Kami == Okitama-no-Kami is Ranked second among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following {{ill|Takimatsuri-no-Kami|ja|滝deity}}. It is located at the northwest corner of the precinct on a stone-paved platform facing west. During the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} in June and December and the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭}} in October, all participants pray earnestly to Okitama-no-Kami for blessings. The enshrined deity, Okitama-no-Kami, is the guardian of Naikū. According to the ''Shinmei Hisyo,'' it is another name for either {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦大神}} or his descendant {{ill|Ōtano-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命}}. In the Kamakura period, Okitama-no-Kami was revered as the land deity of Naikū and played a significant role in offerings during major festivals like Kannamesai and Tsukinami-sai. == Miyabi-no-Kami == Miyabi-no-Kami Ranks third among the subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri-no-Kami and Okitama-no-Kami. It is enshrined on the same platform as Okitama-no-Kami but faces north. Records from the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} indicate that {{ill|Uchimonoi-fu|ja|内物忌父}} offered sacred food during rituals.Sakurai (1991): p. 244 Its location in the northwest contrasts with the southeastern placement of {{ill|Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神}}, where {{ill|Sotomoi-fu|ja|外物忌父}} performed similar duties. The enshrined deity, Miyabi-no-Kami, is believed to be another name for either {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|大宮売命}} or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命}}, the wife of Sarutahiko. == Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami == Ranked fourth among the subordinate shrines, Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is located on the east side of Naikū’s stone staircase, facing south. It is also enshrined on a stone-paved platform outside the {{ill|Itagaki Shrine|ja|板垣}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 114 During the Kamakura period, {{ill|Sotomoi-fu|ja|外物忌父}} offered sacred food during rituals, contrasting with the duties of Uchimonoi-fu at Miyabi-no-Kami. The deity Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is the guardian of the sacred grounds of Naikū. According to the ''Kojiki,'' this deity may be the same as the ''Hahiki-no-Kami,'' a descendant of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami}}, a child of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}. Scholar Sakurai Katsunoshin suggests it is synonymous with ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.''Sakurai (1991): p. 241 == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the ''Enryaku Ritual Book'' of 804 but appear in documents from the late Heian period. The names Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami are consistent, but Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is referred to as ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.'' == Access == To the front of Uji Bridge: * By train: From {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, take the Mie Kōtsū bus from {{ill|Iseshi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station}} to Naikū-mae.Mie Tourism Federation, "Ise Grand Shrine (Naikū)" (accessed September 12, 2013). * By car: From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, exit at Ise West IC or Ise IC and follow signs to parking areas near Naikū. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Gakken Publishing, ''Let’s Visit Ise Grand Shrine,'' supervised by {{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1. * Sakurai Katsunoshin, ''The Development and Archetype of Ise Grand Shrine,'' Kokusho Publishing, 1991. ISBN 4-336-03296-3. * Fukuyama Toshio et al., ''Jingū: The 60th Shikinen Sengū,'' Shogakukan, 1975. * Ise Bunka-sha, ''Pilgrimage to 125 Shrines of Ise,'' Ise Culture Publishing, 2008. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4. == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Shishi Shrine|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami}} * {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0110.html Okitama-no-Kami] - Jingū Association * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0111.html Miyabi-no-Kami] - Jingū Association * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0112.html Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami] - Jingū Association {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Okitama no Kami, Miyabi no Kami, and Yano Wahiki no Kami | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Okitama no Kami & Miyabi no Kami.jpg|270px]]
The ancient shrine site of Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami (2016)
During the {{ill|Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}, the central stone is enshrined as Okitama no Kami. | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, Uji-Kannabe-cho 1, within the Goshōgu (main sanctuary) of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮}} | LatitudeDeg = 34 | LatitudeMin = 30 | LatitudeSec = 43.6 | LongitudeDeg = 136 | LongitudeMin = 44 | LongitudeSec = 12.7 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Okitama no Kami, Miyabi no Kami, and Yano Wahiki no Kami | type = Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} | Established = {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Main Building = None | Alias = | Pilgrimage = | festival = | Rituals = }} [[File:Naiku 001.jpg|250px|thumb|The worship hall of Naikū, where the three shrines are located in the rear]] '''Okitama no Kami''' (興玉神) is one of the subordinate shrines under the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, and the associated deity of the shrine. Located within the inner sanctuary of Naikū, this article also describes {{ill|Miyabi no Kami|ja|宮比神}} and {{ill|Yano Wahiki no Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神}}, two additional subordinate shrines located in the same precinct. All three shrines do not have their own shrine buildings and enshrine their deities directly on stone pavements, serving as protective deities for Naikū.Gakken Publishing (2013): Page 56 == Overview == Located within the inner sanctuary of Naikū in Uji-Kannabe-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. General worshippers cannot enter the inner sanctuary and instead worship from the nearby paths.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): Page 113 During the {{ill|Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}, held every 20 years, the three shrines are also moved along with the main sanctuary.Fukuyama et al. (1975): Page 229 Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami are enshrined on the same stone pavement. Okitama no Kami faces west, while Miyabi no Kami faces north. == Okitama no Kami == Okitama no Kami Ranks second among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following {{ill|Takimatsuri no Kami|ja|滝deity}}. It is enshrined on a west-facing stone pavement located in the northwest corner of the inner sanctuary. During the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} in June and December, as well as the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} in October, those serving the rituals first offer their prayers with full devotion to Okitama no Kami. The enshrined deity, Okitama no Kami (興玉神), is considered the guardian deity of the main sanctuary. According to the *Jinmei Hisho* (神名秘書), Okitama no Kami is another name for {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神}} or his descendant {{ill|Ōta no Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命}}. During the Kamakura period, Okitama no Kami was worshipped as the landowner deity (*jinushi kami*) of the Naikū precincts, and it played a central role in the rituals of the Kannamesai and Tsukinami-no-Matsuri. == Miyabi no Kami == Miyabi no Kami Ranks third among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri no Kami and Okitama no Kami. It is enshrined on a north-facing stone pavement located in the northwest corner of the inner sanctuary. According to records from the {{ill|Kenkū|ja|建久}} era (1190–1198), {{ill|Uchimonoi chichi|ja|内物忌父}} and others were responsible for offering sacred food (*shinsen*).Sakurai (1991): Page 244 The enshrined deity, Miyabi no Kami (宮比神), is considered another guardian deity of the main sanctuary. Miyabi no Kami is believed to be an alias of {{ill|Ōmiyanome no Mikoto|ja|大宮売命}} or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命}}, the wife of Sarutahiko Ōkami. == Yano Wahiki no Kami == Yano Wahiki no Kami Ranks fourth among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri no Kami, Okitama no Kami, and Miyabi no Kami. It is enshrined on the south-facing stone pavement located east of the stone steps of the Goshōgu. Like the other two shrines, it is enshrined on a stone pavement and located outside the Itagaki Gate.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): Page 114 The enshrined deity, Yano Wahiki no Kami (屋乃波比伎神), is considered the guardian deity of the sacred grounds surrounding the main sanctuary. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Yano Wahiki no Kami is mentioned as a descendant of {{ill|Susanoo no Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}} through his son {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami}}. == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the *Enryaku Gishikichō* (延暦儀式帳) from {{ill|Enryaku 23|ja|延暦23rd year}} (804), but their names appear in documents from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. The names Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami remain unchanged, but Yano Wahiki no Kami was historically referred to as "Yano Wahiki no Kami" (矢乃波々岐神). == Access == ; To Uji Bridge: * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} Sangu Line or {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道}} Yamada Line, disembark at {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} (south exit) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station}} and take a Mie Kōtsū bus for approximately 15 minutes to Naikū-mae.Mie Prefecture Tourism Association [http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Shrine (Naikū)] (Accessed September 12, 2013) * Accessible by car from {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} via Ise West IC or Ise IC, with parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okita-no-Kami]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:January]] [[Category:Ise City shrines]] [[Category:興玉神]] }} [[sn:Okita-no-Kami]] [[ja:興玉神]] [[en:Okita-no-Kami]] {{translated page|ja|興玉神|version=84404234|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It is enshrined on the south-facing stone pavement located east of the stone steps of the Goshōgu. Like the other two shrines, it is enshrined on a stone pavement and located outside the Itagaki Gate.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): Page 114 The enshrined deity, Yano Wahiki no Kami (屋乃波比伎神), is considered the guardian deity of the sacred grounds surrounding the main sanctuary. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, Yano Wahiki no Kami is mentioned as a descendant of {{ill|Susanoo no Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft}} through his son {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=draft}}. == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the *Enryaku Gishikichō* (延暦儀式帳) from {{ill|Enryaku 23|ja|延暦23rd year|12=draft}} (804), but their names appear in documents from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}. The names Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami remain unchanged, but Yano Wahiki no Kami was historically referred to as "Yano Wahiki no Kami" (矢乃波々岐神). == Access == ; To Uji Bridge: * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}} Sangu Line or {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|12=draft}} Yamada Line, disembark at {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft}} (south exit) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=draft}} and take a Mie Kōtsū bus for approximately 15 minutes to Naikū-mae.Mie Prefecture Tourism Association [http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Shrine (Naikū)] (Accessed September 12, 2013) * Accessible by car from {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}} via Ise West IC or Ise IC, with parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|興玉神|version=84404234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okita-no-Kami]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:January]] [[Category:Ise City shrines]] [[Category:興玉神]] }} [[sn:Okita-no-Kami]] [[ja:興玉神]] [[en:Okita-no-Kami]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name= Okitama-no-Kami, Miyabi-no-Kami, Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami (興玉神・宮比神・屋乃波比伎神) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Okitama no Kami & Miyabi no Kami.jpg|270px]]
Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami's former shrine site (January 2016)
The central stone becomes the object of worship for Okitama-no-Kami during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. | location=[[Mie Prefecture|Mie]], [[Ise City|Ise]], Uji Kanda-cho 1, within the Naikū Goshōgū | coordinates={{coord|34|30|43.6|N|136|44|12.7|E|region:JP24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Okitama Shrine}} | deity=Okitama-no-Kami: Okitama-no-Kami
Miyabi-no-Kami: Miyabi-no-Kami
Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami: Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami | Classification=Subordinate shrine of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} | consecration_year={{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}} | MainHall=None }} [[File:Naiku 001.jpg|250px|thumb|The worship hall of Naikū (the three shrines are located behind this area)]] '''Okitama-no-Kami''' (興玉神) is one of the guardian deities enshrined in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine is located within the sacred precincts of Naikū.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56 This article also describes {{ill|Miyabi-no-Kami|ja|宮比神|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神|12=draft|12=draft}}, subordinate shrines within the same precinct. All three shrines lack dedicated structures and are guardian shrines of Naikū. == Overview == The shrines are located within the sacred precincts of Naikū in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Mie Prefecture. As these precincts are off-limits to general visitors, worshippers typically pay their respects from designated areas along the {{ill|Sando|ja|参道|12=draft|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 113 During the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, conducted every 20 years, the three shrines are also relocated in accordance with the main shrine’s reconstruction.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 229 Among the three shrines, Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami are enshrined on the same stone-paved platform, with Okitama-no-Kami facing west and Miyabi-no-Kami facing north. == Okitama-no-Kami == Okitama-no-Kami is Ranked second among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following {{ill|Takimatsuri-no-Kami|ja|滝deity|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is located at the northwest corner of the precinct on a stone-paved platform facing west. During the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft|12=draft}} in June and December and the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} in October, all participants pray earnestly to Okitama-no-Kami for blessings. The enshrined deity, Okitama-no-Kami, is the guardian of Naikū. According to the ''Shinmei Hisyo,'' it is another name for either {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦大神|12=draft|12=draft}} or his descendant {{ill|Ōtano-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the Kamakura period, Okitama-no-Kami was revered as the land deity of Naikū and played a significant role in offerings during major festivals like Kannamesai and Tsukinami-sai. == Miyabi-no-Kami == Miyabi-no-Kami Ranks third among the subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri-no-Kami and Okitama-no-Kami. It is enshrined on the same platform as Okitama-no-Kami but faces north. Records from the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft}} indicate that {{ill|Uchimonoi-fu|ja|内物忌父|12=draft|12=draft}} offered sacred food during rituals.Sakurai (1991): p. 244 Its location in the northwest contrasts with the southeastern placement of {{ill|Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神|12=draft|12=draft}}, where {{ill|Sotomoi-fu|ja|外物忌父|12=draft|12=draft}} performed similar duties. The enshrined deity, Miyabi-no-Kami, is believed to be another name for either {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|大宮売命|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the wife of Sarutahiko. == Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami == Ranked fourth among the subordinate shrines, Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is located on the east side of Naikū’s stone staircase, facing south. It is also enshrined on a stone-paved platform outside the {{ill|Itagaki Shrine|ja|板垣|12=draft|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 114 During the Kamakura period, {{ill|Sotomoi-fu|ja|外物忌父|12=draft|12=draft}} offered sacred food during rituals, contrasting with the duties of Uchimonoi-fu at Miyabi-no-Kami. The deity Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is the guardian of the sacred grounds of Naikū. According to the ''Kojiki,'' this deity may be the same as the ''Hahiki-no-Kami,'' a descendant of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=draft|12=draft}}, a child of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft|12=draft}}. Scholar Sakurai Katsunoshin suggests it is synonymous with ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.''Sakurai (1991): p. 241 == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the ''Enryaku Ritual Book'' of 804 but appear in documents from the late Heian period. The names Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami are consistent, but Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is referred to as ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.'' == Access == To the front of Uji Bridge: * By train: From {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}}, take the Mie Kōtsū bus from {{ill|Iseshi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=draft|12=draft}} to Naikū-mae.Mie Tourism Federation, "Ise Grand Shrine (Naikū)" (accessed September 12, 2013). * By car: From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, exit at Ise West IC or Ise IC and follow signs to parking areas near Naikū. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Gakken Publishing, ''Let’s Visit Ise Grand Shrine,'' supervised by {{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔|12=draft|12=draft}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1. * Sakurai Katsunoshin, ''The Development and Archetype of Ise Grand Shrine,'' Kokusho Publishing, 1991. ISBN 4-336-03296-3. * Fukuyama Toshio et al., ''Jingū: The 60th Shikinen Sengū,'' Shogakukan, 1975. * Ise Bunka-sha, ''Pilgrimage to 125 Shrines of Ise,'' Ise Culture Publishing, 2008. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4. == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shishi Shrine|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0110.html Okitama-no-Kami] - Jingū Association * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0111.html Miyabi-no-Kami] - Jingū Association * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0112.html Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami] - Jingū Association {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Okitama no Kami, Miyabi no Kami, and Yano Wahiki no Kami | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Okitama no Kami & Miyabi no Kami.jpg|270px]]
The ancient shrine site of Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami (2016)
During the {{ill|Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, the central stone is enshrined as Okitama no Kami. | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Uji-Kannabe-cho 1, within the Goshōgu (main sanctuary) of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮|12=draft|12=draft}} | LatitudeDeg = 34 | LatitudeMin = 30 | LatitudeSec = 43.6 | LongitudeDeg = 136 | LongitudeMin = 44 | LongitudeSec = 12.7 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Okitama no Kami, Miyabi no Kami, and Yano Wahiki no Kami | type = Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} | Established = {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}} | Main Building = None | Alias = | Pilgrimage = | festival = | Rituals = }} [[File:Naiku 001.jpg|250px|thumb|The worship hall of Naikū, where the three shrines are located in the rear]] '''Okitama no Kami''' (興玉神) is one of the subordinate shrines under the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the associated deity of the shrine. Located within the inner sanctuary of Naikū, this article also describes {{ill|Miyabi no Kami|ja|宮比神|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yano Wahiki no Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神|12=draft|12=draft}}, two additional subordinate shrines located in the same precinct. All three shrines do not have their own shrine buildings and enshrine their deities directly on stone pavements, serving as protective deities for Naikū.Gakken Publishing (2013): Page 56 == Overview == Located within the inner sanctuary of Naikū in Uji-Kannabe-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. General worshippers cannot enter the inner sanctuary and instead worship from the nearby paths.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): Page 113 During the {{ill|Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, held every 20 years, the three shrines are also moved along with the main sanctuary.Fukuyama et al. (1975): Page 229 Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami are enshrined on the same stone pavement. Okitama no Kami faces west, while Miyabi no Kami faces north. == Okitama no Kami == Okitama no Kami Ranks second among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following {{ill|Takimatsuri no Kami|ja|滝deity|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is enshrined on a west-facing stone pavement located in the northwest corner of the inner sanctuary. During the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft|12=draft}} in June and December, as well as the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} in October, those serving the rituals first offer their prayers with full devotion to Okitama no Kami. The enshrined deity, Okitama no Kami (興玉神), is considered the guardian deity of the main sanctuary. According to the *Jinmei Hisho* (神名秘書), Okitama no Kami is another name for {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=draft|12=draft}} or his descendant {{ill|Ōta no Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=draft|12=draft}}. During the Kamakura period, Okitama no Kami was worshipped as the landowner deity (*jinushi kami*) of the Naikū precincts, and it played a central role in the rituals of the Kannamesai and Tsukinami-no-Matsuri. == Miyabi no Kami == Miyabi no Kami Ranks third among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri no Kami and Okitama no Kami. It is enshrined on a north-facing stone pavement located in the northwest corner of the inner sanctuary. According to records from the {{ill|Kenkū|ja|建久|12=draft|12=draft}} era (1190–1198), {{ill|Uchimonoi chichi|ja|内物忌父|12=draft|12=draft}} and others were responsible for offering sacred food (*shinsen*).Sakurai (1991): Page 244 The enshrined deity, Miyabi no Kami (宮比神), is considered another guardian deity of the main sanctuary. Miyabi no Kami is believed to be an alias of {{ill|Ōmiyanome no Mikoto|ja|大宮売命|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the wife of Sarutahiko Ōkami. == Yano Wahiki no Kami == Yano Wahiki no Kami Ranks fourth among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri no Kami, Okitama no Kami, and Miyabi no Kami. It is enshrined on the south-facing stone pavement located east of the stone steps of the Goshōgu. Like the other two shrines, it is enshrined on a stone pavement and located outside the Itagaki Gate.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): Page 114 The enshrined deity, Yano Wahiki no Kami (屋乃波比伎神), is considered the guardian deity of the sacred grounds surrounding the main sanctuary. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yano Wahiki no Kami is mentioned as a descendant of {{ill|Susanoo no Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft|12=draft}} through his son {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the *Enryaku Gishikichō* (延暦儀式帳) from {{ill|Enryaku 23|ja|延暦23rd year|12=draft|12=draft}} (804), but their names appear in documents from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. The names Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami remain unchanged, but Yano Wahiki no Kami was historically referred to as "Yano Wahiki no Kami" (矢乃波々岐神). == Access == ; To Uji Bridge: * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} Sangu Line or {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} Yamada Line, disembark at {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (south exit) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=draft|12=draft}} and take a Mie Kōtsū bus for approximately 15 minutes to Naikū-mae.Mie Prefecture Tourism Association [http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Shrine (Naikū)] (Accessed September 12, 2013) * Accessible by car from {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} via Ise West IC or Ise IC, with parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|興玉神|version=84404234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okita-no-Kami]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:January]] [[Category:Ise City shrines]] [[Category:興玉神]] }} [[sn:Okita-no-Kami]] [[ja:興玉神]] [[en:Okita-no-Kami]]

According to the ''Kojiki,'' this deity may be the same as the ''Hahiki-no-Kami,'' a descendant of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=draft|12=draft}}, a child of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft|12=draft}}. Scholar Sakurai Katsunoshin suggests it is synonymous with ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.''Sakurai (1991): p. 241 == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the ''Enryaku Ritual Book'' of 804 but appear in documents from the late Heian period. The names Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami are consistent, but Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is referred to as ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.'' == Access == To the front of Uji Bridge: * By train: From {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}}, take the Mie Kōtsū bus from {{ill|Iseshi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=draft|12=draft}} to Naikū-mae.Mie Tourism Federation, "Ise Grand Shrine (Naikū)" (accessed September 12, 2013). * By car: From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, exit at Ise West IC or Ise IC and follow signs to parking areas near Naikū. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Gakken Publishing, ''Let’s Visit Ise Grand Shrine,'' supervised by {{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔|12=draft|12=draft}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1. * Sakurai Katsunoshin, ''The Development and Archetype of Ise Grand Shrine,'' Kokusho Publishing, 1991. ISBN 4-336-03296-3. * Fukuyama Toshio et al., ''Jingū: The 60th Shikinen Sengū,'' Shogakukan, 1975. * Ise Bunka-sha, ''Pilgrimage to 125 Shrines of Ise,'' Ise Culture Publishing, 2008. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4. == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shishi Shrine|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0110.html Okitama-no-Kami] - Jingū Association * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0111.html Miyabi-no-Kami] - Jingū Association * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0112.html Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami] - Jingū Association {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Okitama no Kami, Miyabi no Kami, and Yano Wahiki no Kami | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Okitama no Kami & Miyabi no Kami.jpg|270px]]
The ancient shrine site of Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami (2016)
During the {{ill|Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, the central stone is enshrined as Okitama no Kami. | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Uji-Kannabe-cho 1, within the Goshōgu (main sanctuary) of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮|12=draft|12=draft}} | LatitudeDeg = 34 | LatitudeMin = 30 | LatitudeSec = 43.6 | LongitudeDeg = 136 | LongitudeMin = 44 | LongitudeSec = 12.7 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Okitama no Kami, Miyabi no Kami, and Yano Wahiki no Kami | type = Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} | Established = {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}} | Main Building = None | Alias = | Pilgrimage = | festival = | Rituals = }} [[File:Naiku 001.jpg|250px|thumb|The worship hall of Naikū, where the three shrines are located in the rear]] '''Okitama no Kami''' (興玉神) is one of the subordinate shrines under the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the associated deity of the shrine. Located within the inner sanctuary of Naikū, this article also describes {{ill|Miyabi no Kami|ja|宮比神|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yano Wahiki no Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神|12=draft|12=draft}}, two additional subordinate shrines located in the same precinct. All three shrines do not have their own shrine buildings and enshrine their deities directly on stone pavements, serving as protective deities for Naikū.Gakken Publishing (2013): Page 56 == Overview == Located within the inner sanctuary of Naikū in Uji-Kannabe-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. General worshippers cannot enter the inner sanctuary and instead worship from the nearby paths.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): Page 113 During the {{ill|Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, held every 20 years, the three shrines are also moved along with the main sanctuary.Fukuyama et al. (1975): Page 229 Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami are enshrined on the same stone pavement. Okitama no Kami faces west, while Miyabi no Kami faces north. == Okitama no Kami == Okitama no Kami Ranks second among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following {{ill|Takimatsuri no Kami|ja|滝deity|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is enshrined on a west-facing stone pavement located in the northwest corner of the inner sanctuary. During the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft|12=draft}} in June and December, as well as the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} in October, those serving the rituals first offer their prayers with full devotion to Okitama no Kami. The enshrined deity, Okitama no Kami (興玉神), is considered the guardian deity of the main sanctuary. According to the *Jinmei Hisho* (神名秘書), Okitama no Kami is another name for {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=draft|12=draft}} or his descendant {{ill|Ōta no Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=draft|12=draft}}. During the Kamakura period, Okitama no Kami was worshipped as the landowner deity (*jinushi kami*) of the Naikū precincts, and it played a central role in the rituals of the Kannamesai and Tsukinami-no-Matsuri. == Miyabi no Kami == Miyabi no Kami Ranks third among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri no Kami and Okitama no Kami. It is enshrined on a north-facing stone pavement located in the northwest corner of the inner sanctuary. According to records from the {{ill|Kenkū|ja|建久|12=draft|12=draft}} era (1190–1198), {{ill|Uchimonoi chichi|ja|内物忌父|12=draft|12=draft}} and others were responsible for offering sacred food (*shinsen*).Sakurai (1991): Page 244 The enshrined deity, Miyabi no Kami (宮比神), is considered another guardian deity of the main sanctuary. Miyabi no Kami is believed to be an alias of {{ill|Ōmiyanome no Mikoto|ja|大宮売命|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the wife of Sarutahiko Ōkami. == Yano Wahiki no Kami == Yano Wahiki no Kami Ranks fourth among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri no Kami, Okitama no Kami, and Miyabi no Kami. It is enshrined on the south-facing stone pavement located east of the stone steps of the Goshōgu. Like the other two shrines, it is enshrined on a stone pavement and located outside the Itagaki Gate.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): Page 114 The enshrined deity, Yano Wahiki no Kami (屋乃波比伎神), is considered the guardian deity of the sacred grounds surrounding the main sanctuary. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yano Wahiki no Kami is mentioned as a descendant of {{ill|Susanoo no Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft|12=draft}} through his son {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the *Enryaku Gishikichō* (延暦儀式帳) from {{ill|Enryaku 23|ja|延暦23rd year|12=draft|12=draft}} (804), but their names appear in documents from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. The names Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami remain unchanged, but Yano Wahiki no Kami was historically referred to as "Yano Wahiki no Kami" (矢乃波々岐神). == Access == ; To Uji Bridge: * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} Sangu Line or {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} Yamada Line, disembark at {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (south exit) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=draft|12=draft}} and take a Mie Kōtsū bus for approximately 15 minutes to Naikū-mae.Mie Prefecture Tourism Association [http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Shrine (Naikū)] (Accessed September 12, 2013) * Accessible by car from {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} via Ise West IC or Ise IC, with parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|興玉神|version=84404234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okita-no-Kami]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:January]] [[Category:Ise City shrines]] [[Category:興玉神]] }} [[sn:Okita-no-Kami]] [[ja:興玉神]] [[en:Okita-no-Kami]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name= Okitama-no-Kami, Miyabi-no-Kami, Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami (興玉神・宮比神・屋乃波比伎神) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Okitama no Kami & Miyabi no Kami.jpg|270px]]
Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami's former shrine site (January 2016)
The central stone becomes the object of worship for Okitama-no-Kami during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. | location=[[Mie Prefecture|Mie]], [[Ise City|Ise]], Uji Kanda-cho 1, within the Naikū Goshōgū | coordinates={{coord|34|30|43.6|N|136|44|12.7|E|region:JP24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Okitama Shrine}} | deity=Okitama-no-Kami: Okitama-no-Kami
Miyabi-no-Kami: Miyabi-no-Kami
Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami: Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami | Classification=Subordinate shrine of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | consecration_year={{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | MainHall=None }} [[File:Naiku 001.jpg|250px|thumb|The worship hall of Naikū (the three shrines are located behind this area)]] '''Okitama-no-Kami''' (興玉神) is one of the guardian deities enshrined in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine is located within the sacred precincts of Naikū.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56 This article also describes {{ill|Miyabi-no-Kami|ja|宮比神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, subordinate shrines within the same precinct. All three shrines lack dedicated structures and are guardian shrines of Naikū. == Overview == The shrines are located within the sacred precincts of Naikū in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Mie Prefecture. As these precincts are off-limits to general visitors, worshippers typically pay their respects from designated areas along the {{ill|Sando|ja|参道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 113 During the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, conducted every 20 years, the three shrines are also relocated in accordance with the main shrine’s reconstruction.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 229 Among the three shrines, Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami are enshrined on the same stone-paved platform, with Okitama-no-Kami facing west and Miyabi-no-Kami facing north. == Okitama-no-Kami == Okitama-no-Kami is Ranked second among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following {{ill|Takimatsuri-no-Kami|ja|滝deity|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is located at the northwest corner of the precinct on a stone-paved platform facing west. During the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in June and December and the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in October, all participants pray earnestly to Okitama-no-Kami for blessings. The enshrined deity, Okitama-no-Kami, is the guardian of Naikū. According to the ''Shinmei Hisyo,'' it is another name for either {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or his descendant {{ill|Ōtano-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the Kamakura period, Okitama-no-Kami was revered as the land deity of Naikū and played a significant role in offerings during major festivals like Kannamesai and Tsukinami-sai. == Miyabi-no-Kami == Miyabi-no-Kami Ranks third among the subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri-no-Kami and Okitama-no-Kami. It is enshrined on the same platform as Okitama-no-Kami but faces north. Records from the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} indicate that {{ill|Uchimonoi-fu|ja|内物忌父|qq=draft|qq=draft}} offered sacred food during rituals.Sakurai (1991): p. 244 Its location in the northwest contrasts with the southeastern placement of {{ill|Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where {{ill|Sotomoi-fu|ja|外物忌父|qq=draft|qq=draft}} performed similar duties. The enshrined deity, Miyabi-no-Kami, is believed to be another name for either {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|大宮売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the wife of Sarutahiko. == Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami == Ranked fourth among the subordinate shrines, Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is located on the east side of Naikū’s stone staircase, facing south. It is also enshrined on a stone-paved platform outside the {{ill|Itagaki Shrine|ja|板垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 114 During the Kamakura period, {{ill|Sotomoi-fu|ja|外物忌父|qq=draft|qq=draft}} offered sacred food during rituals, contrasting with the duties of Uchimonoi-fu at Miyabi-no-Kami. The deity Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is the guardian of the sacred grounds of Naikū. According to the ''Kojiki,'' this deity may be the same as the ''Hahiki-no-Kami,'' a descendant of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a child of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Scholar Sakurai Katsunoshin suggests it is synonymous with ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.''Sakurai (1991): p. 241 == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the ''Enryaku Ritual Book'' of 804 but appear in documents from the late Heian period. The names Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami are consistent, but Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is referred to as ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.'' == Access == To the front of Uji Bridge: * By train: From {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the Mie Kōtsū bus from {{ill|Iseshi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to Naikū-mae.Mie Tourism Federation, "Ise Grand Shrine (Naikū)" (accessed September 12, 2013). * By car: From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, exit at Ise West IC or Ise IC and follow signs to parking areas near Naikū. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Gakken Publishing, ''Let’s Visit Ise Grand Shrine,'' supervised by {{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013.
