It is enshrined on the south-facing stone pavement located east of the stone steps of the Goshōgu. Like the other two shrines, it is enshrined on a stone pavement and located outside the Itagaki Gate.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): Page 114 The enshrined deity, Yano Wahiki no Kami (屋乃波比伎神), is considered the guardian deity of the sacred grounds surrounding the main sanctuary. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yano Wahiki no Kami is mentioned as a descendant of {{ill|Susanoo no Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} through his son {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the *Enryaku Gishikichō* (延暦儀式帳) from {{ill|Enryaku 23|ja|延暦23rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (804), but their names appear in documents from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The names Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami remain unchanged, but Yano Wahiki no Kami was historically referred to as "Yano Wahiki no Kami" (矢乃波々岐神). == Access == ; To Uji Bridge: * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Sangu Line or {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Yamada Line, disembark at {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (south exit) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and take a Mie Kōtsū bus for approximately 15 minutes to Naikū-mae.Mie Prefecture Tourism Association [http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Shrine (Naikū)] (Accessed September 12, 2013) * Accessible by car from {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via Ise West IC or Ise IC, with parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|興玉神|version=84404234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okita-no-Kami]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:January]] [[Category:Ise City shrines]] [[Category:興玉神]] }} [[sn:Okita-no-Kami]] [[ja:興玉神]] [[en:Okita-no-Kami]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name= Okitama-no-Kami, Miyabi-no-Kami, Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami (興玉神・宮比神・屋乃波比伎神) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Okitama no Kami & Miyabi no Kami.jpg|270px]]
Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami's former shrine site (January 2016)
The central stone becomes the object of worship for Okitama-no-Kami during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. | location=[[Mie Prefecture|Mie]], [[Ise City|Ise]], Uji Kanda-cho 1, within the Naikū Goshōgū | coordinates={{coord|34|30|43.6|N|136|44|12.7|E|region:JP24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Okitama Shrine}} | deity=Okitama-no-Kami: Okitama-no-Kami
Miyabi-no-Kami: Miyabi-no-Kami
Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami: Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami | Classification=Subordinate shrine of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | consecration_year={{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | MainHall=None }} [[File:Naiku 001.jpg|250px|thumb|The worship hall of Naikū (the three shrines are located behind this area)]] '''Okitama-no-Kami''' (興玉神) is one of the guardian deities enshrined in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine is located within the sacred precincts of Naikū.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56 This article also describes {{ill|Miyabi-no-Kami|ja|宮比神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, subordinate shrines within the same precinct. All three shrines lack dedicated structures and are guardian shrines of Naikū. == Overview == The shrines are located within the sacred precincts of Naikū in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Mie Prefecture. As these precincts are off-limits to general visitors, worshippers typically pay their respects from designated areas along the {{ill|Sando|ja|参道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 113 During the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, conducted every 20 years, the three shrines are also relocated in accordance with the main shrine’s reconstruction.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 229 Among the three shrines, Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami are enshrined on the same stone-paved platform, with Okitama-no-Kami facing west and Miyabi-no-Kami facing north. == Okitama-no-Kami == Okitama-no-Kami is Ranked second among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following {{ill|Takimatsuri-no-Kami|ja|滝deity|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is located at the northwest corner of the precinct on a stone-paved platform facing west. During the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in June and December and the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in October, all participants pray earnestly to Okitama-no-Kami for blessings. The enshrined deity, Okitama-no-Kami, is the guardian of Naikū. According to the ''Shinmei Hisyo,'' it is another name for either {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or his descendant {{ill|Ōtano-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the Kamakura period, Okitama-no-Kami was revered as the land deity of Naikū and played a significant role in offerings during major festivals like Kannamesai and Tsukinami-sai. == Miyabi-no-Kami == Miyabi-no-Kami Ranks third among the subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri-no-Kami and Okitama-no-Kami. It is enshrined on the same platform as Okitama-no-Kami but faces north. Records from the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} indicate that {{ill|Uchimonoi-fu|ja|内物忌父|qq=draft|qq=draft}} offered sacred food during rituals.Sakurai (1991): p. 244 Its location in the northwest contrasts with the southeastern placement of {{ill|Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where {{ill|Sotomoi-fu|ja|外物忌父|qq=draft|qq=draft}} performed similar duties. The enshrined deity, Miyabi-no-Kami, is believed to be another name for either {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|大宮売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the wife of Sarutahiko. == Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami == Ranked fourth among the subordinate shrines, Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is located on the east side of Naikū’s stone staircase, facing south. It is also enshrined on a stone-paved platform outside the {{ill|Itagaki Shrine|ja|板垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 114 During the Kamakura period, {{ill|Sotomoi-fu|ja|外物忌父|qq=draft|qq=draft}} offered sacred food during rituals, contrasting with the duties of Uchimonoi-fu at Miyabi-no-Kami. The deity Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is the guardian of the sacred grounds of Naikū. According to the ''Kojiki,'' this deity may be the same as the ''Hahiki-no-Kami,'' a descendant of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a child of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Scholar Sakurai Katsunoshin suggests it is synonymous with ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.''Sakurai (1991): p. 241 == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the ''Enryaku Ritual Book'' of 804 but appear in documents from the late Heian period. The names Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami are consistent, but Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is referred to as ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.'' == Access == To the front of Uji Bridge: * By train: From {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the Mie Kōtsū bus from {{ill|Iseshi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to Naikū-mae.Mie Tourism Federation, "Ise Grand Shrine (Naikū)" (accessed September 12, 2013). * By car: From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, exit at Ise West IC or Ise IC and follow signs to parking areas near Naikū. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Gakken Publishing, ''Let’s Visit Ise Grand Shrine,'' supervised by {{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1. * Sakurai Katsunoshin, ''The Development and Archetype of Ise Grand Shrine,'' Kokusho Publishing, 1991. ISBN 4-336-03296-3. * Fukuyama Toshio et al., ''Jingū: The 60th Shikinen Sengū,'' Shogakukan, 1975. * Ise Bunka-sha, ''Pilgrimage to 125 Shrines of Ise,'' Ise Culture Publishing, 2008. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4. == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shishi Shrine|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0110.html Okitama-no-Kami] - Jingū Association * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0111.html Miyabi-no-Kami] - Jingū Association * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0112.html Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami] - Jingū Association {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Okitama no Kami, Miyabi no Kami, and Yano Wahiki no Kami | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Okitama no Kami & Miyabi no Kami.jpg|270px]]
The ancient shrine site of Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami (2016)
During the {{ill|Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the central stone is enshrined as Okitama no Kami. | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Uji-Kannabe-cho 1, within the Goshōgu (main sanctuary) of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | LatitudeDeg = 34 | LatitudeMin = 30 | LatitudeSec = 43.6 | LongitudeDeg = 136 | LongitudeMin = 44 | LongitudeSec = 12.7 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Okitama no Kami, Miyabi no Kami, and Yano Wahiki no Kami | type = Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Established = {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Main Building = None | Alias = | Pilgrimage = | festival = | Rituals = }} [[File:Naiku 001.jpg|250px|thumb|The worship hall of Naikū, where the three shrines are located in the rear]] '''Okitama no Kami''' (興玉神) is one of the subordinate shrines under the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the associated deity of the shrine. Located within the inner sanctuary of Naikū, this article also describes {{ill|Miyabi no Kami|ja|宮比神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yano Wahiki no Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, two additional subordinate shrines located in the same precinct. All three shrines do not have their own shrine buildings and enshrine their deities directly on stone pavements, serving as protective deities for Naikū.Gakken Publishing (2013): Page 56 == Overview == Located within the inner sanctuary of Naikū in Uji-Kannabe-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. General worshippers cannot enter the inner sanctuary and instead worship from the nearby paths.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): Page 113 During the {{ill|Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, held every 20 years, the three shrines are also moved along with the main sanctuary.Fukuyama et al. (1975): Page 229 Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami are enshrined on the same stone pavement. Okitama no Kami faces west, while Miyabi no Kami faces north. == Okitama no Kami == Okitama no Kami Ranks second among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following {{ill|Takimatsuri no Kami|ja|滝deity|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is enshrined on a west-facing stone pavement located in the northwest corner of the inner sanctuary. During the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in June and December, as well as the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in October, those serving the rituals first offer their prayers with full devotion to Okitama no Kami. The enshrined deity, Okitama no Kami (興玉神), is considered the guardian deity of the main sanctuary. According to the *Jinmei Hisho* (神名秘書), Okitama no Kami is another name for {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or his descendant {{ill|Ōta no Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the Kamakura period, Okitama no Kami was worshipped as the landowner deity (*jinushi kami*) of the Naikū precincts, and it played a central role in the rituals of the Kannamesai and Tsukinami-no-Matsuri. == Miyabi no Kami == Miyabi no Kami Ranks third among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri no Kami and Okitama no Kami. It is enshrined on a north-facing stone pavement located in the northwest corner of the inner sanctuary. According to records from the {{ill|Kenkū|ja|建久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} era (1190–1198), {{ill|Uchimonoi chichi|ja|内物忌父|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others were responsible for offering sacred food (*shinsen*).Sakurai (1991): Page 244 The enshrined deity, Miyabi no Kami (宮比神), is considered another guardian deity of the main sanctuary. Miyabi no Kami is believed to be an alias of {{ill|Ōmiyanome no Mikoto|ja|大宮売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the wife of Sarutahiko Ōkami. == Yano Wahiki no Kami == Yano Wahiki no Kami Ranks fourth among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri no Kami, Okitama no Kami, and Miyabi no Kami. It is enshrined on the south-facing stone pavement located east of the stone steps of the Goshōgu. Like the other two shrines, it is enshrined on a stone pavement and located outside the Itagaki Gate.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): Page 114 The enshrined deity, Yano Wahiki no Kami (屋乃波比伎神), is considered the guardian deity of the sacred grounds surrounding the main sanctuary. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yano Wahiki no Kami is mentioned as a descendant of {{ill|Susanoo no Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} through his son {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the *Enryaku Gishikichō* (延暦儀式帳) from {{ill|Enryaku 23|ja|延暦23rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (804), but their names appear in documents from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The names Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami remain unchanged, but Yano Wahiki no Kami was historically referred to as "Yano Wahiki no Kami" (矢乃波々岐神). == Access == ; To Uji Bridge: * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Sangu Line or {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Yamada Line, disembark at {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (south exit) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and take a Mie Kōtsū bus for approximately 15 minutes to Naikū-mae.Mie Prefecture Tourism Association [http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Shrine (Naikū)] (Accessed September 12, 2013) * Accessible by car from {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via Ise West IC or Ise IC, with parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|興玉神|version=84404234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okita-no-Kami]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:January]] [[Category:Ise City shrines]] [[Category:興玉神]] }} [[sn:Okita-no-Kami]] [[ja:興玉神]] [[en:Okita-no-Kami]]

The deity Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is the guardian of the sacred grounds of Naikū. According to the ''Kojiki,'' this deity may be the same as the ''Hahiki-no-Kami,'' a descendant of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a child of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Scholar Sakurai Katsunoshin suggests it is synonymous with ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.''Sakurai (1991): p. 241 == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the ''Enryaku Ritual Book'' of 804 but appear in documents from the late Heian period. The names Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami are consistent, but Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is referred to as ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.'' == Access == To the front of Uji Bridge: * By train: From {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the Mie Kōtsū bus from {{ill|Iseshi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to Naikū-mae.Mie Tourism Federation, "Ise Grand Shrine (Naikū)" (accessed September 12, 2013). * By car: From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, exit at Ise West IC or Ise IC and follow signs to parking areas near Naikū. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Gakken Publishing, ''Let’s Visit Ise Grand Shrine,'' supervised by {{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1. * Sakurai Katsunoshin, ''The Development and Archetype of Ise Grand Shrine,'' Kokusho Publishing, 1991. ISBN 4-336-03296-3. * Fukuyama Toshio et al., ''Jingū: The 60th Shikinen Sengū,'' Shogakukan, 1975. * Ise Bunka-sha, ''Pilgrimage to 125 Shrines of Ise,'' Ise Culture Publishing, 2008. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4. == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shishi Shrine|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0110.html Okitama-no-Kami] - Jingū Association * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0111.html Miyabi-no-Kami] - Jingū Association * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0112.html Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami] - Jingū Association {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Okitama no Kami, Miyabi no Kami, and Yano Wahiki no Kami | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Okitama no Kami & Miyabi no Kami.jpg|270px]]
The ancient shrine site of Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami (2016)
During the {{ill|Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the central stone is enshrined as Okitama no Kami. | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Uji-Kannabe-cho 1, within the Goshōgu (main sanctuary) of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | LatitudeDeg = 34 | LatitudeMin = 30 | LatitudeSec = 43.6 | LongitudeDeg = 136 | LongitudeMin = 44 | LongitudeSec = 12.7 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Okitama no Kami, Miyabi no Kami, and Yano Wahiki no Kami | type = Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Established = {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Main Building = None | Alias = | Pilgrimage = | festival = | Rituals = }} [[File:Naiku 001.jpg|250px|thumb|The worship hall of Naikū, where the three shrines are located in the rear]] '''Okitama no Kami''' (興玉神) is one of the subordinate shrines under the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the associated deity of the shrine. Located within the inner sanctuary of Naikū, this article also describes {{ill|Miyabi no Kami|ja|宮比神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yano Wahiki no Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, two additional subordinate shrines located in the same precinct. All three shrines do not have their own shrine buildings and enshrine their deities directly on stone pavements, serving as protective deities for Naikū.Gakken Publishing (2013): Page 56 == Overview == Located within the inner sanctuary of Naikū in Uji-Kannabe-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. General worshippers cannot enter the inner sanctuary and instead worship from the nearby paths.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): Page 113 During the {{ill|Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, held every 20 years, the three shrines are also moved along with the main sanctuary.Fukuyama et al. (1975): Page 229 Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami are enshrined on the same stone pavement. Okitama no Kami faces west, while Miyabi no Kami faces north. == Okitama no Kami == Okitama no Kami Ranks second among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following {{ill|Takimatsuri no Kami|ja|滝deity|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is enshrined on a west-facing stone pavement located in the northwest corner of the inner sanctuary. During the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in June and December, as well as the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in October, those serving the rituals first offer their prayers with full devotion to Okitama no Kami. The enshrined deity, Okitama no Kami (興玉神), is considered the guardian deity of the main sanctuary. According to the *Jinmei Hisho* (神名秘書), Okitama no Kami is another name for {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or his descendant {{ill|Ōta no Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the Kamakura period, Okitama no Kami was worshipped as the landowner deity (*jinushi kami*) of the Naikū precincts, and it played a central role in the rituals of the Kannamesai and Tsukinami-no-Matsuri. == Miyabi no Kami == Miyabi no Kami Ranks third among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri no Kami and Okitama no Kami. It is enshrined on a north-facing stone pavement located in the northwest corner of the inner sanctuary. According to records from the {{ill|Kenkū|ja|建久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} era (1190–1198), {{ill|Uchimonoi chichi|ja|内物忌父|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others were responsible for offering sacred food (*shinsen*).Sakurai (1991): Page 244 The enshrined deity, Miyabi no Kami (宮比神), is considered another guardian deity of the main sanctuary. Miyabi no Kami is believed to be an alias of {{ill|Ōmiyanome no Mikoto|ja|大宮売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the wife of Sarutahiko Ōkami. == Yano Wahiki no Kami == Yano Wahiki no Kami Ranks fourth among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri no Kami, Okitama no Kami, and Miyabi no Kami. It is enshrined on the south-facing stone pavement located east of the stone steps of the Goshōgu. Like the other two shrines, it is enshrined on a stone pavement and located outside the Itagaki Gate.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): Page 114 The enshrined deity, Yano Wahiki no Kami (屋乃波比伎神), is considered the guardian deity of the sacred grounds surrounding the main sanctuary. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yano Wahiki no Kami is mentioned as a descendant of {{ill|Susanoo no Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} through his son {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the *Enryaku Gishikichō* (延暦儀式帳) from {{ill|Enryaku 23|ja|延暦23rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (804), but their names appear in documents from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The names Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami remain unchanged, but Yano Wahiki no Kami was historically referred to as "Yano Wahiki no Kami" (矢乃波々岐神). == Access == ; To Uji Bridge: * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Sangu Line or {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Yamada Line, disembark at {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (south exit) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and take a Mie Kōtsū bus for approximately 15 minutes to Naikū-mae.Mie Prefecture Tourism Association [http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Shrine (Naikū)] (Accessed September 12, 2013) * Accessible by car from {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via Ise West IC or Ise IC, with parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|興玉神|version=84404234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okita-no-Kami]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:January]] [[Category:Ise City shrines]] [[Category:興玉神]] }} [[sn:Okita-no-Kami]] [[ja:興玉神]] [[en:Okita-no-Kami]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name= Okitama-no-Kami, Miyabi-no-Kami, Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami (興玉神・宮比神・屋乃波比伎神) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Okitama no Kami & Miyabi no Kami.jpg|270px]]
Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami's former shrine site (January 2016)
The central stone becomes the object of worship for Okitama-no-Kami during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. | location=[[Mie Prefecture|Mie]], [[Ise City|Ise]], Uji Kanda-cho 1, within the Naikū Goshōgū | coordinates={{coord|34|30|43.6|N|136|44|12.7|E|region:JP24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Okitama Shrine}} | deity=Okitama-no-Kami: Okitama-no-Kami
Miyabi-no-Kami: Miyabi-no-Kami
Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami: Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami | Classification=Subordinate shrine of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | consecration_year={{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | MainHall=None }} [[File:Naiku 001.jpg|250px|thumb|The worship hall of Naikū (the three shrines are located behind this area)]] '''Okitama-no-Kami''' (興玉神) is one of the guardian deities enshrined in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine is located within the sacred precincts of Naikū.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56 This article also describes {{ill|Miyabi-no-Kami|ja|宮比神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, subordinate shrines within the same precinct. All three shrines lack dedicated structures and are guardian shrines of Naikū. == Overview == The shrines are located within the sacred precincts of Naikū in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Mie Prefecture. As these precincts are off-limits to general visitors, worshippers typically pay their respects from designated areas along the {{ill|Sando|ja|参道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 113 During the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, conducted every 20 years, the three shrines are also relocated in accordance with the main shrine’s reconstruction.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 229 Among the three shrines, Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami are enshrined on the same stone-paved platform, with Okitama-no-Kami facing west and Miyabi-no-Kami facing north. == Okitama-no-Kami == Okitama-no-Kami is Ranked second among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following {{ill|Takimatsuri-no-Kami|ja|滝deity|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is located at the northwest corner of the precinct on a stone-paved platform facing west. During the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in June and December and the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in October, all participants pray earnestly to Okitama-no-Kami for blessings. The enshrined deity, Okitama-no-Kami, is the guardian of Naikū. According to the ''Shinmei Hisyo,'' it is another name for either {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or his descendant {{ill|Ōtano-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the Kamakura period, Okitama-no-Kami was revered as the land deity of Naikū and played a significant role in offerings during major festivals like Kannamesai and Tsukinami-sai. == Miyabi-no-Kami == Miyabi-no-Kami Ranks third among the subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri-no-Kami and Okitama-no-Kami. It is enshrined on the same platform as Okitama-no-Kami but faces north. Records from the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} indicate that {{ill|Uchimonoi-fu|ja|内物忌父|qq=draft|qq=draft}} offered sacred food during rituals.Sakurai (1991): p. 244 Its location in the northwest contrasts with the southeastern placement of {{ill|Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where {{ill|Sotomoi-fu|ja|外物忌父|qq=draft|qq=draft}} performed similar duties. The enshrined deity, Miyabi-no-Kami, is believed to be another name for either {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|大宮売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the wife of Sarutahiko. == Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami == Ranked fourth among the subordinate shrines, Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is located on the east side of Naikū’s stone staircase, facing south. It is also enshrined on a stone-paved platform outside the {{ill|Itagaki Shrine|ja|板垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 114 During the Kamakura period, {{ill|Sotomoi-fu|ja|外物忌父|qq=draft|qq=draft}} offered sacred food during rituals, contrasting with the duties of Uchimonoi-fu at Miyabi-no-Kami. The deity Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is the guardian of the sacred grounds of Naikū. According to the ''Kojiki,'' this deity may be the same as the ''Hahiki-no-Kami,'' a descendant of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a child of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Scholar Sakurai Katsunoshin suggests it is synonymous with ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.''Sakurai (1991): p. 241 == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the ''Enryaku Ritual Book'' of 804 but appear in documents from the late Heian period. The names Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami are consistent, but Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is referred to as ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.'' == Access == To the front of Uji Bridge: * By train: From {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the Mie Kōtsū bus from {{ill|Iseshi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to Naikū-mae.Mie Tourism Federation, "Ise Grand Shrine (Naikū)" (accessed September 12, 2013). * By car: From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, exit at Ise West IC or Ise IC and follow signs to parking areas near Naikū. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Gakken Publishing, ''Let’s Visit Ise Grand Shrine,'' supervised by {{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. It is enshrined on the south-facing stone pavement located east of the stone steps of the Goshōgu. Like the other two shrines, it is enshrined on a stone pavement and located outside the Itagaki Gate.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): Page 114 The enshrined deity, Yano Wahiki no Kami (屋乃波比伎神), is considered the guardian deity of the sacred grounds surrounding the main sanctuary. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yano Wahiki no Kami is mentioned as a descendant of {{ill|Susanoo no Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} through his son {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the *Enryaku Gishikichō* (延暦儀式帳) from {{ill|Enryaku 23|ja|延暦23rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (804), but their names appear in documents from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The names Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami remain unchanged, but Yano Wahiki no Kami was historically referred to as "Yano Wahiki no Kami" (矢乃波々岐神). == Access == ; To Uji Bridge: * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Sangu Line or {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Yamada Line, disembark at {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (south exit) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and take a Mie Kōtsū bus for approximately 15 minutes to Naikū-mae.Mie Prefecture Tourism Association [http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Shrine (Naikū)] (Accessed September 12, 2013) * Accessible by car from {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via Ise West IC or Ise IC, with parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|興玉神|version=84404234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okita-no-Kami]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:January]] [[Category:Ise City shrines]] }} [[sn:Okita-no-Kami]] [[ja:興玉神]] [[en:Okita-no-Kami]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name= Okitama-no-Kami, Miyabi-no-Kami, Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami (興玉神・宮比神・屋乃波比伎神) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Okitama no Kami & Miyabi no Kami.jpg|270px]]
Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami's former shrine site (January 2016)
The central stone becomes the object of worship for Okitama-no-Kami during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. | location=[[Mie Prefecture|Mie]], [[Ise City|Ise]], Uji Kanda-cho 1, within the Naikū Goshōgū | coordinates={{coord|34|30|43.6|N|136|44|12.7|E|region:JP24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Okitama Shrine}} | deity=Okitama-no-Kami: Okitama-no-Kami
Miyabi-no-Kami: Miyabi-no-Kami
Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami: Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami | Classification=Subordinate shrine of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | consecration_year={{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | MainHall=None }} [[File:Naiku 001.jpg|250px|thumb|The worship hall of Naikū (the three shrines are located behind this area)]] '''Okitama-no-Kami''' (興玉神) is one of the guardian deities enshrined in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine is located within the sacred precincts of Naikū.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56 This article also describes {{ill|Miyabi-no-Kami|ja|宮比神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, subordinate shrines within the same precinct. All three shrines lack dedicated structures and are guardian shrines of Naikū. == Overview == The shrines are located within the sacred precincts of Naikū in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Mie Prefecture. As these precincts are off-limits to general visitors, worshippers typically pay their respects from designated areas along the {{ill|Sando|ja|参道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 113 During the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, conducted every 20 years, the three shrines are also relocated in accordance with the main shrine’s reconstruction.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 229 Among the three shrines, Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami are enshrined on the same stone-paved platform, with Okitama-no-Kami facing west and Miyabi-no-Kami facing north. == Okitama-no-Kami == Okitama-no-Kami is Ranked second among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following {{ill|Takimatsuri-no-Kami|ja|滝deity|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is located at the northwest corner of the precinct on a stone-paved platform facing west. During the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in June and December and the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in October, all participants pray earnestly to Okitama-no-Kami for blessings. The enshrined deity, Okitama-no-Kami, is the guardian of Naikū. According to the ''Shinmei Hisyo,'' it is another name for either {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or his descendant {{ill|Ōtano-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the Kamakura period, Okitama-no-Kami was revered as the land deity of Naikū and played a significant role in offerings during major festivals like Kannamesai and Tsukinami-sai. == Miyabi-no-Kami == Miyabi-no-Kami Ranks third among the subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri-no-Kami and Okitama-no-Kami. It is enshrined on the same platform as Okitama-no-Kami but faces north. Records from the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} indicate that {{ill|Uchimonoi-fu|ja|内物忌父|qq=draft|qq=draft}} offered sacred food during rituals.Sakurai (1991): p. 244 Its location in the northwest contrasts with the southeastern placement of {{ill|Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where {{ill|Sotomoi-fu|ja|外物忌父|qq=draft|qq=draft}} performed similar duties. The enshrined deity, Miyabi-no-Kami, is believed to be another name for either {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|大宮売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the wife of Sarutahiko. == Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami == Ranked fourth among the subordinate shrines, Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is located on the east side of Naikū’s stone staircase, facing south. It is also enshrined on a stone-paved platform outside the {{ill|Itagaki Shrine|ja|板垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 114 During the Kamakura period, {{ill|Sotomoi-fu|ja|外物忌父|qq=draft|qq=draft}} offered sacred food during rituals, contrasting with the duties of Uchimonoi-fu at Miyabi-no-Kami. The deity Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is the guardian of the sacred grounds of Naikū. According to the ''Kojiki,'' this deity may be the same as the ''Hahiki-no-Kami,'' a descendant of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a child of {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Scholar Sakurai Katsunoshin suggests it is synonymous with ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.''Sakurai (1991): p. 241 == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the ''Enryaku Ritual Book'' of 804 but appear in documents from the late Heian period. The names Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami are consistent, but Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is referred to as ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.'' == Access == To the front of Uji Bridge: * By train: From {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the Mie Kōtsū bus from {{ill|Iseshi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to Naikū-mae.Mie Tourism Federation, "Ise Grand Shrine (Naikū)" (accessed September 12, 2013). * By car: From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, exit at Ise West IC or Ise IC and follow signs to parking areas near Naikū. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Gakken Publishing, ''Let’s Visit Ise Grand Shrine,'' supervised by {{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1. * Sakurai Katsunoshin, ''The Development and Archetype of Ise Grand Shrine,'' Kokusho Publishing, 1991. ISBN 4-336-03296-3. * Fukuyama Toshio et al., ''Jingū: The 60th Shikinen Sengū,'' Shogakukan, 1975. * Ise Bunka-sha, ''Pilgrimage to 125 Shrines of Ise,'' Ise Culture Publishing, 2008. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4. == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shishi Shrine|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0110.html Okitama-no-Kami] - Jingū Association * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0111.html Miyabi-no-Kami] - Jingū Association * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0112.html Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami] - Jingū Association {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Okitama no Kami, Miyabi no Kami, and Yano Wahiki no Kami | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Okitama no Kami & Miyabi no Kami.jpg|270px]]
The ancient shrine site of Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami (2016)
During the {{ill|Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the central stone is enshrined as Okitama no Kami. | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Uji-Kannabe-cho 1, within the Goshōgu (main sanctuary) of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | LatitudeDeg = 34 | LatitudeMin = 30 | LatitudeSec = 43.6 | LongitudeDeg = 136 | LongitudeMin = 44 | LongitudeSec = 12.7 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Okitama no Kami, Miyabi no Kami, and Yano Wahiki no Kami | type = Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Established = {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Main Building = None | Alias = | Pilgrimage = | festival = | Rituals = }} [[File:Naiku 001.jpg|250px|thumb|The worship hall of Naikū, where the three shrines are located in the rear]] '''Okitama no Kami''' (興玉神) is one of the subordinate shrines under the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the associated deity of the shrine. Located within the inner sanctuary of Naikū, this article also describes {{ill|Miyabi no Kami|ja|宮比神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yano Wahiki no Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, two additional subordinate shrines located in the same precinct. All three shrines do not have their own shrine buildings and enshrine their deities directly on stone pavements, serving as protective deities for Naikū.Gakken Publishing (2013): Page 56 == Overview == Located within the inner sanctuary of Naikū in Uji-Kannabe-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. General worshippers cannot enter the inner sanctuary and instead worship from the nearby paths.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): Page 113 During the {{ill|Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, held every 20 years, the three shrines are also moved along with the main sanctuary.Fukuyama et al. (1975): Page 229 Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami are enshrined on the same stone pavement. Okitama no Kami faces west, while Miyabi no Kami faces north. == Okitama no Kami == Okitama no Kami Ranks second among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following {{ill|Takimatsuri no Kami|ja|滝deity|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is enshrined on a west-facing stone pavement located in the northwest corner of the inner sanctuary. During the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in June and December, as well as the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in October, those serving the rituals first offer their prayers with full devotion to Okitama no Kami. The enshrined deity, Okitama no Kami (興玉神), is considered the guardian deity of the main sanctuary. According to the *Jinmei Hisho* (神名秘書), Okitama no Kami is another name for {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or his descendant {{ill|Ōta no Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the Kamakura period, Okitama no Kami was worshipped as the landowner deity (*jinushi kami*) of the Naikū precincts, and it played a central role in the rituals of the Kannamesai and Tsukinami-no-Matsuri. == Miyabi no Kami == Miyabi no Kami Ranks third among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri no Kami and Okitama no Kami. It is enshrined on a north-facing stone pavement located in the northwest corner of the inner sanctuary. According to records from the {{ill|Kenkū|ja|建久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} era (1190–1198), {{ill|Uchimonoi chichi|ja|内物忌父|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others were responsible for offering sacred food (*shinsen*).Sakurai (1991): Page 244 The enshrined deity, Miyabi no Kami (宮比神), is considered another guardian deity of the main sanctuary. Miyabi no Kami is believed to be an alias of {{ill|Ōmiyanome no Mikoto|ja|大宮売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the wife of Sarutahiko Ōkami. == Yano Wahiki no Kami == Yano Wahiki no Kami Ranks fourth among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri no Kami, Okitama no Kami, and Miyabi no Kami. It is enshrined on the south-facing stone pavement located east of the stone steps of the Goshōgu. Like the other two shrines, it is enshrined on a stone pavement and located outside the Itagaki Gate.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): Page 114 The enshrined deity, Yano Wahiki no Kami (屋乃波比伎神), is considered the guardian deity of the sacred grounds surrounding the main sanctuary. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yano Wahiki no Kami is mentioned as a descendant of {{ill|Susanoo no Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} through his son {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the *Enryaku Gishikichō* (延暦儀式帳) from {{ill|Enryaku 23|ja|延暦23rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (804), but their names appear in documents from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The names Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami remain unchanged, but Yano Wahiki no Kami was historically referred to as "Yano Wahiki no Kami" (矢乃波々岐神). == Access == ; To Uji Bridge: * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Sangu Line or {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Yamada Line, disembark at {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (south exit) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and take a Mie Kōtsū bus for approximately 15 minutes to Naikū-mae.Mie Prefecture Tourism Association [http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Shrine (Naikū)] (Accessed September 12, 2013) * Accessible by car from {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via Ise West IC or Ise IC, with parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|興玉神|version=84404234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okita-no-Kami]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:January]] [[Category:Ise City shrines]] }} [[sn:Okita-no-Kami]] [[ja:興玉神]] [[en:Okita-no-Kami]]

The shrine is located within the sacred precincts of Naikū.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56 This article also describes {{ill|Miyabi-no-Kami|ja|宮比神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami|qq=}}, subordinate shrines within the same precinct. As these precincts are off-limits to general visitors, worshippers typically pay their respects from designated areas along the {{ill|Sando|ja|参道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sando|qq=}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 113 During the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, conducted every 20 years, the three shrines are also relocated in accordance with the main shrine’s reconstruction.Fukuyama et al. It is located at the northwest corner of the precinct on a stone-paved platform facing west. During the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} in June and December and the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai Festival|qq=}} in October, all participants pray earnestly to Okitama-no-Kami for blessings. In the Kamakura period, Okitama-no-Kami was revered as the land deity of Naikū and played a significant role in offerings during major festivals like Kannamesai and Tsukinami-sai. == Miyabi-no-Kami == Miyabi-no-Kami Ranks third among the subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri-no-Kami and Okitama-no-Kami. It is enshrined on the same platform as Okitama-no-Kami but faces north. Records from the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}} indicate that {{ill|Uchimonoi-fu|ja|内物忌父|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimonoi-fu|qq=}} offered sacred food during rituals.Sakurai (1991): p. 244 Its location in the northwest contrasts with the southeastern placement of {{ill|Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami|qq=}}, where {{ill|Sotomoi-fu|ja|外物忌父|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotomoi-fu|qq=}} performed similar duties. The enshrined deity, Miyabi-no-Kami, is believed to be another name for either {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|大宮売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|qq=}}, the wife of Sarutahiko. == Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami == Ranked fourth among the subordinate shrines, Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is located on the east side of Naikū’s stone staircase, facing south. It is also enshrined on a stone-paved platform outside the {{ill|Itagaki Shrine|ja|板垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itagaki Shrine|qq=}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 114 During the Kamakura period, {{ill|Sotomoi-fu|ja|外物忌父|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotomoi-fu|qq=}} offered sacred food during rituals, contrasting with the duties of Uchimonoi-fu at Miyabi-no-Kami. The deity Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is the guardian of the sacred grounds of Naikū. According to the ''Kojiki,'' this deity may be the same as the ''Hahiki-no-Kami,'' a descendant of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}, a child of {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Scholar Sakurai Katsunoshin suggests it is synonymous with ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.''Sakurai (1991): p. 241 == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the ''Enryaku Ritual Book'' of 804 but appear in documents from the late Heian period. The names Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami are consistent, but Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is referred to as ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.'' == Access == To the front of Uji Bridge: * By train: From {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Sangū Line|qq=}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Yamada Line|qq=}}, take the Mie Kōtsū bus from {{ill|Iseshi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iseshi Station|qq=}} or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|qq=}} to Naikū-mae.Mie Tourism Federation, "Ise Grand Shrine (Naikū)" (accessed September 12, 2013). * By car: From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}, exit at Ise West IC or Ise IC and follow signs to parking areas near Naikū. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Gakken Publishing, ''Let’s Visit Ise Grand Shrine,'' supervised by {{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonoda Minoru|qq=}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1. * Sakurai Katsunoshin, ''The Development and Archetype of Ise Grand Shrine,'' Kokusho Publishing, 1991. ISBN 4-336-03296-3. * Fukuyama Toshio et al., ''Jingū: The 60th Shikinen Sengū,'' Shogakukan, 1975. * Ise Bunka-sha, ''Pilgrimage to 125 Shrines of Ise,'' Ise Culture Publishing, 2008. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4. == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Shishi Shrine|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futami Okitama Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0110.html Okitama-no-Kami] - Jingū Association * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0111.html Miyabi-no-Kami] - Jingū Association * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0112.html Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami] - Jingū Association {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Okitama no Kami, Miyabi no Kami, and Yano Wahiki no Kami | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Okitama no Kami & Miyabi no Kami.jpg|270px]]
The ancient shrine site of Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami (2016)
During the {{ill|Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinen Sengu|qq=}}, the central stone is enshrined as Okitama no Kami. | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise|qq=}}, Uji-Kannabe-cho 1, within the Goshōgu (main sanctuary) of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikū|qq=}} | LatitudeDeg = 34 | LatitudeMin = 30 | LatitudeSec = 43.6 | LongitudeDeg = 136 | LongitudeMin = 44 | LongitudeSec = 12.7 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Okitama no Kami, Miyabi no Kami, and Yano Wahiki no Kami | type = Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} | Established = {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}} | Main Building = None | Alias = | Pilgrimage = | festival = | Rituals = }} [[File:Naiku 001.jpg|250px|thumb|The worship hall of Naikū, where the three shrines are located in the rear]] '''Okitama no Kami''' (興玉神) is one of the subordinate shrines under the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Jingū|qq=}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City|qq=}}, and the associated deity of the shrine. Located within the inner sanctuary of Naikū, this article also describes {{ill|Miyabi no Kami|ja|宮比神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabi no Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Yano Wahiki no Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano Wahiki no Kami|qq=}}, two additional subordinate shrines located in the same precinct. All three shrines do not have their own shrine buildings and enshrine their deities directly on stone pavements, serving as protective deities for Naikū.Gakken Publishing (2013): Page 56 == Overview == Located within the inner sanctuary of Naikū in Uji-Kannabe-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. General worshippers cannot enter the inner sanctuary and instead worship from the nearby paths.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): Page 113 During the {{ill|Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinen Sengu|qq=}}, held every 20 years, the three shrines are also moved along with the main sanctuary.Fukuyama et al. (1975): Page 229 Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami are enshrined on the same stone pavement. Okitama no Kami faces west, while Miyabi no Kami faces north. == Okitama no Kami == Okitama no Kami Ranks second among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following {{ill|Takimatsuri no Kami|ja|滝deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takimatsuri no Kami|qq=}}. It is enshrined on a west-facing stone pavement located in the northwest corner of the inner sanctuary. During the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} in June and December, as well as the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} in October, those serving the rituals first offer their prayers with full devotion to Okitama no Kami. The enshrined deity, Okitama no Kami (興玉神), is considered the guardian deity of the main sanctuary. According to the *Jinmei Hisho* (神名秘書), Okitama no Kami is another name for {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}} or his descendant {{ill|Ōta no Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. During the Kamakura period, Okitama no Kami was worshipped as the landowner deity (*jinushi kami*) of the Naikū precincts, and it played a central role in the rituals of the Kannamesai and Tsukinami-no-Matsuri. == Miyabi no Kami == Miyabi no Kami Ranks third among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri no Kami and Okitama no Kami. It is enshrined on a north-facing stone pavement located in the northwest corner of the inner sanctuary. According to records from the {{ill|Kenkū|ja|建久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkū|qq=}} era (1190–1198), {{ill|Uchimonoi chichi|ja|内物忌父|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimonoi chichi|qq=}} and others were responsible for offering sacred food (*shinsen*).Sakurai (1991): Page 244 The enshrined deity, Miyabi no Kami (宮比神), is considered another guardian deity of the main sanctuary. Miyabi no Kami is believed to be an alias of {{ill|Ōmiyanome no Mikoto|ja|大宮売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome no Mikoto|qq=}} or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|qq=}}, the wife of Sarutahiko Ōkami. == Yano Wahiki no Kami == Yano Wahiki no Kami Ranks fourth among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri no Kami, Okitama no Kami, and Miyabi no Kami. It is enshrined on the south-facing stone pavement located east of the stone steps of the Goshōgu. Like the other two shrines, it is enshrined on a stone pavement and located outside the Itagaki Gate.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): Page 114 The enshrined deity, Yano Wahiki no Kami (屋乃波比伎神), is considered the guardian deity of the sacred grounds surrounding the main sanctuary. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, Yano Wahiki no Kami is mentioned as a descendant of {{ill|Susanoo no Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo no Mikoto|qq=}} through his son {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}. == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the *Enryaku Gishikichō* (延暦儀式帳) from {{ill|Enryaku 23|ja|延暦23rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku 23|qq=}} (804), but their names appear in documents from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}. The names Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami remain unchanged, but Yano Wahiki no Kami was historically referred to as "Yano Wahiki no Kami" (矢乃波々岐神). == Access == ; To Uji Bridge: * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR|qq=}} Sangu Line or {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu|qq=}} Yamada Line, disembark at {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Station|qq=}} (south exit) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|qq=}} and take a Mie Kōtsū bus for approximately 15 minutes to Naikū-mae.Mie Prefecture Tourism Association [http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Shrine (Naikū)] (Accessed September 12, 2013) * Accessible by car from {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}} via Ise West IC or Ise IC, with parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|興玉神|version=84404234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okita-no-Kami]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:January]] [[Category:Ise City shrines]] }} [[sn:Okita-no-Kami]] [[ja:興玉神]] [[en:Okita-no-Kami]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] to [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name= Okitama-no-Kami, Miyabi-no-Kami, Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami (興玉神・宮比神・屋乃波比伎神) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Okitama no Kami & Miyabi no Kami.jpg|270px]]
Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami's former shrine site (January 2016)
The central stone becomes the object of worship for Okitama-no-Kami during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}. | location=[[Mie Prefecture|Mie]], [[Ise City|Ise]], Uji Kanda-cho 1, within the Naikū Goshōgū | coordinates={{coord|34|30|43.6|N|136|44|12.7|E|region:JP24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Okitama Shrine}} | deity=Okitama-no-Kami: Okitama-no-Kami
Miyabi-no-Kami: Miyabi-no-Kami
Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami: Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami | Classification=Subordinate shrine of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} | consecration_year={{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}} | MainHall=None }} [[File:Naiku 001.jpg|250px|thumb|The worship hall of Naikū (the three shrines are located behind this area)]] '''Okitama-no-Kami''' (興玉神) is one of the guardian deities enshrined in the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}. The shrine is located within the sacred precincts of Naikū.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56 This article also describes {{ill|Miyabi-no-Kami|ja|宮比神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami|qq=}}, subordinate shrines within the same precinct. All three shrines lack dedicated structures and are guardian shrines of Naikū. == Overview == The shrines are located within the sacred precincts of Naikū in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City|qq=}}, Mie Prefecture. As these precincts are off-limits to general visitors, worshippers typically pay their respects from designated areas along the {{ill|Sando|ja|参道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sando|qq=}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 113 During the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, conducted every 20 years, the three shrines are also relocated in accordance with the main shrine’s reconstruction.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 229 Among the three shrines, Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami are enshrined on the same stone-paved platform, with Okitama-no-Kami facing west and Miyabi-no-Kami facing north. == Okitama-no-Kami == Okitama-no-Kami is Ranked second among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following {{ill|Takimatsuri-no-Kami|ja|滝deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takimatsuri-no-Kami|qq=}}. It is located at the northwest corner of the precinct on a stone-paved platform facing west. During the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} in June and December and the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai Festival|qq=}} in October, all participants pray earnestly to Okitama-no-Kami for blessings. The enshrined deity, Okitama-no-Kami, is the guardian of Naikū. According to the ''Shinmei Hisyo,'' it is another name for either {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}} or his descendant {{ill|Ōtano-Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtano-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. In the Kamakura period, Okitama-no-Kami was revered as the land deity of Naikū and played a significant role in offerings during major festivals like Kannamesai and Tsukinami-sai. == Miyabi-no-Kami == Miyabi-no-Kami Ranks third among the subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri-no-Kami and Okitama-no-Kami. It is enshrined on the same platform as Okitama-no-Kami but faces north. Records from the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}} indicate that {{ill|Uchimonoi-fu|ja|内物忌父|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimonoi-fu|qq=}} offered sacred food during rituals.Sakurai (1991): p. 244 Its location in the northwest contrasts with the southeastern placement of {{ill|Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami|qq=}}, where {{ill|Sotomoi-fu|ja|外物忌父|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotomoi-fu|qq=}} performed similar duties. The enshrined deity, Miyabi-no-Kami, is believed to be another name for either {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|ja|大宮売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya-no-Me|qq=}} or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|qq=}}, the wife of Sarutahiko. == Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami == Ranked fourth among the subordinate shrines, Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is located on the east side of Naikū’s stone staircase, facing south. It is also enshrined on a stone-paved platform outside the {{ill|Itagaki Shrine|ja|板垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itagaki Shrine|qq=}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 114 During the Kamakura period, {{ill|Sotomoi-fu|ja|外物忌父|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotomoi-fu|qq=}} offered sacred food during rituals, contrasting with the duties of Uchimonoi-fu at Miyabi-no-Kami. The deity Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is the guardian of the sacred grounds of Naikū. According to the ''Kojiki,'' this deity may be the same as the ''Hahiki-no-Kami,'' a descendant of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}, a child of {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Scholar Sakurai Katsunoshin suggests it is synonymous with ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.''Sakurai (1991): p. 241 == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the ''Enryaku Ritual Book'' of 804 but appear in documents from the late Heian period. The names Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami are consistent, but Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami is referred to as ''Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami.'' == Access == To the front of Uji Bridge: * By train: From {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Sangū Line|qq=}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Yamada Line|qq=}}, take the Mie Kōtsū bus from {{ill|Iseshi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iseshi Station|qq=}} or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|qq=}} to Naikū-mae.Mie Tourism Federation, "Ise Grand Shrine (Naikū)" (accessed September 12, 2013). * By car: From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}, exit at Ise West IC or Ise IC and follow signs to parking areas near Naikū. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Gakken Publishing, ''Let’s Visit Ise Grand Shrine,'' supervised by {{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonoda Minoru|qq=}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1. * Sakurai Katsunoshin, ''The Development and Archetype of Ise Grand Shrine,'' Kokusho Publishing, 1991. ISBN 4-336-03296-3. * Fukuyama Toshio et al., ''Jingū: The 60th Shikinen Sengū,'' Shogakukan, 1975. * Ise Bunka-sha, ''Pilgrimage to 125 Shrines of Ise,'' Ise Culture Publishing, 2008. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4. == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Shishi Shrine|ja|四至神|1=Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomeguri-no-Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futami Okitama Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0110.html Okitama-no-Kami] - Jingū Association * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0111.html Miyabi-no-Kami] - Jingū Association * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0112.html Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami] - Jingū Association {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Okitama no Kami, Miyabi no Kami, and Yano Wahiki no Kami | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Okitama no Kami & Miyabi no Kami.jpg|270px]]
The ancient shrine site of Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami (2016)
During the {{ill|Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinen Sengu|qq=}}, the central stone is enshrined as Okitama no Kami. | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise|qq=}}, Uji-Kannabe-cho 1, within the Goshōgu (main sanctuary) of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikū|qq=}} | LatitudeDeg = 34 | LatitudeMin = 30 | LatitudeSec = 43.6 | LongitudeDeg = 136 | LongitudeMin = 44 | LongitudeSec = 12.7 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Okitama no Kami, Miyabi no Kami, and Yano Wahiki no Kami | type = Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} | Established = {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}} | Main Building = None | Alias = | Pilgrimage = | festival = | Rituals = }} [[File:Naiku 001.jpg|250px|thumb|The worship hall of Naikū, where the three shrines are located in the rear]] '''Okitama no Kami''' (興玉神) is one of the subordinate shrines under the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Jingū|qq=}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City|qq=}}, and the associated deity of the shrine. Located within the inner sanctuary of Naikū, this article also describes {{ill|Miyabi no Kami|ja|宮比神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabi no Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Yano Wahiki no Kami|ja|屋乃波比伎神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano Wahiki no Kami|qq=}}, two additional subordinate shrines located in the same precinct. All three shrines do not have their own shrine buildings and enshrine their deities directly on stone pavements, serving as protective deities for Naikū.Gakken Publishing (2013): Page 56 == Overview == Located within the inner sanctuary of Naikū in Uji-Kannabe-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. General worshippers cannot enter the inner sanctuary and instead worship from the nearby paths.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): Page 113 During the {{ill|Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinen Sengu|qq=}}, held every 20 years, the three shrines are also moved along with the main sanctuary.Fukuyama et al. (1975): Page 229 Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami are enshrined on the same stone pavement. Okitama no Kami faces west, while Miyabi no Kami faces north. == Okitama no Kami == Okitama no Kami Ranks second among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following {{ill|Takimatsuri no Kami|ja|滝deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takimatsuri no Kami|qq=}}. It is enshrined on a west-facing stone pavement located in the northwest corner of the inner sanctuary. During the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} in June and December, as well as the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} in October, those serving the rituals first offer their prayers with full devotion to Okitama no Kami. The enshrined deity, Okitama no Kami (興玉神), is considered the guardian deity of the main sanctuary. According to the *Jinmei Hisho* (神名秘書), Okitama no Kami is another name for {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}} or his descendant {{ill|Ōta no Mikoto|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. During the Kamakura period, Okitama no Kami was worshipped as the landowner deity (*jinushi kami*) of the Naikū precincts, and it played a central role in the rituals of the Kannamesai and Tsukinami-no-Matsuri. == Miyabi no Kami == Miyabi no Kami Ranks third among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri no Kami and Okitama no Kami. It is enshrined on a north-facing stone pavement located in the northwest corner of the inner sanctuary. According to records from the {{ill|Kenkū|ja|建久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkū|qq=}} era (1190–1198), {{ill|Uchimonoi chichi|ja|内物忌父|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimonoi chichi|qq=}} and others were responsible for offering sacred food (*shinsen*).Sakurai (1991): Page 244 The enshrined deity, Miyabi no Kami (宮比神), is considered another guardian deity of the main sanctuary. Miyabi no Kami is believed to be an alias of {{ill|Ōmiyanome no Mikoto|ja|大宮売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome no Mikoto|qq=}} or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|qq=}}, the wife of Sarutahiko Ōkami. == Yano Wahiki no Kami == Yano Wahiki no Kami Ranks fourth among the 30 subordinate shrines of Naikū, following Takimatsuri no Kami, Okitama no Kami, and Miyabi no Kami. It is enshrined on the south-facing stone pavement located east of the stone steps of the Goshōgu. Like the other two shrines, it is enshrined on a stone pavement and located outside the Itagaki Gate.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): Page 114 The enshrined deity, Yano Wahiki no Kami (屋乃波比伎神), is considered the guardian deity of the sacred grounds surrounding the main sanctuary. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, Yano Wahiki no Kami is mentioned as a descendant of {{ill|Susanoo no Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo no Mikoto|qq=}} through his son {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}. == History == The shrines are not mentioned in the *Enryaku Gishikichō* (延暦儀式帳) from {{ill|Enryaku 23|ja|延暦23rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku 23|qq=}} (804), but their names appear in documents from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}. The names Okitama no Kami and Miyabi no Kami remain unchanged, but Yano Wahiki no Kami was historically referred to as "Yano Wahiki no Kami" (矢乃波々岐神). == Access == ; To Uji Bridge: * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR|qq=}} Sangu Line or {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu|qq=}} Yamada Line, disembark at {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Station|qq=}} (south exit) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|qq=}} and take a Mie Kōtsū bus for approximately 15 minutes to Naikū-mae.Mie Prefecture Tourism Association [http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Shrine (Naikū)] (Accessed September 12, 2013) * Accessible by car from {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}} via Ise West IC or Ise IC, with parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|興玉神|version=84404234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okita-no-Kami]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:January]] [[Category:Ise City shrines]] }} [[sn:Okita-no-Kami]] [[ja:興玉神]] [[en:Okita-no-Kami]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name= Okitama-no-Kami, Miyabi-no-Kami, Yano-Hahiki-no-Kami (興玉神・宮比神・屋乃波比伎神) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Okitama no Kami & Miyabi no Kami.jpg|270px]]
Okitama-no-Kami and Miyabi-no-Kami's former shrine site (January 2016)
The central stone becomes the object of worship for Okitama-no-Kami during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}. | location=[[Mie Prefecture|Mie]], [[Ise City|Ise]], Uji Kanda-cho 1, within the Naikū Goshōgū | coordinates={{coord|34|30|43.6|N|136|44|12.7|E|region:JP24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Okitama Shrine}} | deity=Okitama-no-Kami: Okitama-no-Kami
Miyabi-no-Kami: Miyabi-no-Kami
